questions.questionText: The variation of displacement (d) with time (t) in the case of a particle falling freely under gravity from rest is correctly shown in:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. For free fall from rest, **d = gt**, showing **displacement time**. The d"t graph is a **parabola opening upward** from the origin.
Graph (b) correctly represents this **parabolic relationship**, unlike (c) which shows a linear relation (constant velocity). Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: A liquid is flowing in a streamlined manner through a cylindrical pipe. Along a section containing the axis of the pipe, the flow profile will be:
questions.questionText: A liquid is flowing in a streamlined manner through a cylindrical pipe. Along a section containing the axis of the pipe, the flow profile will be:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. The flow is **laminar (or streamlined)** and governed by viscosity. The **no-slip condition** makes the velocity zero at the pipe wall.
**Velocity increases toward the center**, forming a parabolic profile. The **maximum velocity occurs at the center**, and zero velocity at the walls.
Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: A truck, a car, and a motorcycle have equal kinetic energies. If equal retarding forces are applied, their stopping distances will be in the ratio:
(Let x, y, z be the stopping distances for truck, car, motorcycle respectively)
questions.questionText: A truck, a car, and a motorcycle have equal kinetic energies. If equal retarding forces are applied, their stopping distances will be in the ratio: (Let x, y, z be the stopping distances for truck, car, motorcycle respectively)
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. The **Work-Energy Theorem** states that the work done by a force equals the change in kinetic energy.
**W = F-d = KE**. Since all vehicles have **equal kinetic energies** and are stopped by **equal retarding forces**, the **stopping distances (d) must be equal**.
Therefore, **x = y = z**. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: When an air bubble at the bottom of a lake rises to the top, it will
questions.questionText: When an air bubble at the bottom of a lake rises to the top, it will
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. This phenomenon is governed by **Boyles Law**, which states that pressure and volume are inversely proportional for a fixed amount of gas at constant temperature (**PV = PV**).
At the bottom of the lake, **pressure is high** due to the water column above. As the bubble rises, **pressure decreases, causing its volume to increase**.
The bubble therefore **expands** as it rises. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Distance-time graph in respect of a race among four persons is shown in the given figure. Consider the following statements in this regard:
1. 'A' stood first in the race.
2. 'C' led all the way.
3. 'D' ran faster than others in the later part of the race.
Of these statements:
questions.questionText: Distance-time graph in respect of a race among four persons is shown in the given figure. Consider the following statements in this regard: 1. 'A' stood first in the race. 2. 'C' led all the way. 3. 'D' ran faster than others in the later part of the race. Of these statements:

options.a: 1 and 3 are false and 2 is true
options.b: 1 and 2 are false and 3 is true
options.c: 1 and 3 are true and 2 is false
options.d: 1 is true and 2 and 3 are false

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. In a distance-time graph, the person who **covers the total distance in the shortest time** wins the race.
Statement 1 is false. The graph shows that person **'A' takes the longest time** to finish the race, while 'D' finishes in the shortest time.
Statement 2 is false. To lead all the way means to be ahead at all times. The graph shows that **'C' was behind 'A' and 'B' initially** and only led later.
Statement 3 is true. The slope of a distance-time graph represents speed. In the later part of the race, **'D' has the steepest slope**, showing the highest speed.
Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: An air bubble in water will act like a "
questions.questionText: An air bubble in water will act like a "
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. The air bubble is surrounded by water, a denser medium. The **refractive index of air (1)** is **less than that of water (1.33)**.
When light passes from a denser to a rarer medium (water ' air), rays **bend away from the normal**. As a result, the **air bubble diverges light rays** passing through it.
A lens that diverges light rays is known as a **concave lens**. Thats why the air bubble acts like a concave lens. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Difference between a nuclear reactor and an atomic bomb
questions.questionText: Difference between a nuclear reactor and an atomic bomb

options.a: No chain reaction in reactor but in bomb
options.b: Chain reaction in reactor is controlled
options.c: Chain reaction in reactor is not controlled
options.d: No chain reaction in bomb while reactor has it

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. Both devices rely on **nuclear fission chain reactions**. In a **nuclear reactor the chain reaction is controlled** using control rods, moderators and design for steady power.
In an **atomic bomb the chain reaction is uncontrolled**, extremely rapid, producing massive energy. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: The variations in temperatures from 0C to 100C with respect to time of two liquids P, Q are shown in the graph given below.
Which one of the following statements is correct?
questions.questionText: The variations in temperatures from 0C to 100C with respect to time of two liquids P, Q are shown in the graph given below. Which one of the following statements is correct?

options.a: During heating, liquid P remained hotter than liquid Q throughout
options.b: At no point of time during heating did the two liquids have the same temperature
options.c: P attained the temperature of 100C faster than Q
options.d: Q attained the temperature of 100C faster than P

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. Graph shows **P is hotter than Q initially** until an intersection point. At the intersection, both liquids have the **same temperature** (so (b) is wrong). After the intersection, **Q becomes hotter and reaches 100C earlier than P** (slope/time to 100C is smaller for Q).
Therefore **Q attains 100C faster**. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Suppose a rocketship is receding from the Earth at speed = 2/10 c. A light in the rocket appears blue to passengers. What colour does it appear to an observer on Earth?
questions.questionText: Suppose a rocketship is receding from the Earth at speed = 2/10 c. A light in the rocket appears blue to passengers. What colour does it appear to an observer on Earth?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Source is receding**, so observed wavelength on Earth is **red-shifted** (wavelength increases).
Using the given relation _obs = _source / (1-v/c) with v/c = 0.2: **factor = 1 / (1-0.2) = 1.25**. **Blue light range 450"475 nm**. Multiply by 1.25 ' **562.5"593.75 nm**. This range falls in the **yellow-orange region** of the visible spectrum.
Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Optical fibre works on the principle of
questions.questionText: Optical fibre works on the principle of
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. An optical fiber is a thin, flexible strand of glass or plastic that acts as a waveguide to transmit light between its two ends.
It works on the principle of **total internal reflection (TIR)**. The fiber consists of a **central core with a high refractive index**, surrounded by a **cladding with a slightly lower refractive index**.
Light is sent into the core at a shallow angle. When the light hits the boundary between the core and the cladding, it **strikes at an angle greater than the critical angle**, causing it to be **completely reflected back** into the core.
This process repeats itself, with the light bouncing off the internal walls of the fiber, allowing it to **travel long distances with very little loss of signal**.
Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Who among the following anticipated Newton by declaring that all things gravitate to the earth?
questions.questionText: Who among the following anticipated Newton by declaring that all things gravitate to the earth?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. Long before Isaac Newton formulated his universal law of gravitation, several ancient and medieval scholars had ideas about gravity.
The 7th-century **Indian mathematician and astronomer Brahmagupta** wrote in his text, the **Brahmasphutasiddhanta**, that "all things fall to the earth by a law of nature, for it is the nature of the earth to attract and keep things." This statement is a remarkable early description of **gravity as an attractive property of the Earth**, anticipating Newton's concept by over a thousand years, although it lacked the mathematical formulation and universality of Newton's law.
Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Assertion (A): A diamond sparkles more than a glass imitation cut to the same shape.
Reason (R): The refractive index of diamond is less than that of glass.
In the context of the above two statements,
which one of the following is correct?
questions.questionText: Assertion (A): A diamond sparkles more than a glass imitation cut to the same shape. Reason (R): The refractive index of diamond is less than that of glass. In the context of the above two statements, which one of the following is correct?

options.a: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A
options.b: Both A and R are true, but R is not a correct explanation of A
options.c: A is true, but R is false
options.d: A is false, but R is true

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Assertion (A) is true**. A well-cut diamond famously **sparkles with brilliance and "fire"** (dispersion of light into colors) far more than a piece of glass cut to the same shape.
**Reason (R) is false**. The sparkle of a diamond is due to its **very high refractive index** (about 2.42) and high dispersion. The high refractive index leads to a **small critical angle**, causing a large amount of **total internal reflection**, which traps light inside the gem and makes it "sparkle".
Glass has a much lower refractive index (about 1.5). The statement that the refractive index of diamond is less than glass is incorrect. Since the assertion is true but the reason is false, the correct option is (c). Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: When the same note is played on a sitar and a flute, the sound produced can be distinguished from each other because of the difference in
questions.questionText: When the same note is played on a sitar and a flute, the sound produced can be distinguished from each other because of the difference in
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. Sound has three main perceptual attributes: **Pitch**: Determined by the fundamental frequency of the sound wave. Playing the "same note" means the **fundamental frequency**, and therefore the pitch, **is the same** for both instruments.
**Loudness**: Determined by the amplitude (intensity) of the sound wave. The instruments **could be played at the same loudness**. **Quality (or Timbre)**: This is what allows us to distinguish between two different instruments playing the same note at the same loudness. It is determined by the **presence and relative intensity of overtones or harmonics** (multiples of the fundamental frequency) produced by the instrument.
Since the note (pitch) is the same, and we can assume they could be played at the same loudness, the distinguishing characteristic is the **quality or timbre**.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Which one of the following can be used to confirm whether drinking water contains a gammaemitting isotope or not?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following can be used to confirm whether drinking water contains a gammaemitting isotope or not?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Gammayays** are a form of high-energy electromagnetic radiation emitted by radioactive isotopes. They **cannot be seen with a microscope**.
A **scintillation counter is a specific type of radiation detector**. It works by using a material (a scintillator) that **emits a flash of light (a scintillation) when it is struck by ionizing radiation**, such as a gammayay.
A **photosensor then detects this flash of light** and converts it into an electrical pulse, which can be counted. This allows for the **detection and measurement of gammayadiation**.
A lead plate is used for shielding against radiation, not detecting it. A spectrophotometer measures the intensity of light, but not ionizing radiation.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Information and Communication Technology, Robotics, AI
statementLines: The technique used to transmit audio signals in television broadcasts is
questions.questionText: The technique used to transmit audio signals in television broadcasts is
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. Analog television broadcasting uses different modulation techniques for its video and audio components to **avoid interference**.
The **video signal** (picture) is typically transmitted using **Amplitude Modulation (AM)**. The **audio signal** (sound) is transmitted using **Frequency Modulation (FM)**.
Using FM for the audio provides **better sound quality** and is **less susceptible to noise** and interference compared to AM. Pulse Code Modulation (PCM) is a digital technique, and Time Division Multiplexing (TDM) is a method for combining multiple signals, not a modulation technique itself.
Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: A parachutist jumps from a height of 5000 metres. The relationship between his falling speed 'v' and the distance fallen through 'd' is best represented as
questions.questionText: A parachutist jumps from a height of 5000 metres. The relationship between his falling speed 'v' and the distance fallen through 'd' is best represented as
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. This question describes the motion of a falling object subject to **air resistance**. **Initially (small d)**: As the parachutist starts falling, their speed (v) **increases due to gravity**. At low speeds, air resistance is small. The speed increases rapidly at first.
**As speed increases**: **Air resistance increases** (proportional to the square of the speed). This opposing force counteracts gravity, causing the **rate of acceleration to decrease**. The speed continues to increase, but more slowly.
**Terminal Velocity**: Eventually, the upward force of air resistance becomes equal in magnitude to the downward force of gravity. At this point, the **net force is zero**, and the parachutist **stops accelerating**. They continue to fall at a constant maximum speed called **terminal velocity**.
Graph (a) correctly represents this relationship: the speed (v) increases with distance (d) at first, but the rate of increase slows down until the **speed becomes constant** (the flat part of the curve), representing terminal velocity.
Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: A meteor is
questions.questionText: A meteor is

options.b: A piece of matter which has entered the earth's atmosphere from outer space

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. A **meteoroid** is a small rocky or metallic body traveling through outer space. When a meteoroid **enters the Earth's atmosphere at high speed**, friction with the air causes it to heat up and incandesce, creating a **streak of light** in the sky.
This visible phenomenon is called a **meteor, or a "shooting star"**. Therefore, a meteor is the **visible path of a piece of matter which has entered the earth's atmosphere** from outer space.
If any part of it survives the journey and hits the ground, it is called a meteorite. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: The colour of a star is an indication of its
questions.questionText: The colour of a star is an indication of its
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. A stars colour corresponds to its **surface temperature** (black-body radiation). **Hotter stars** emit more at **shorter wavelengths ' appear blue/white**. **Cooler stars** emit more at **longer wavelengths ' appear red/orange**.
Hence, the **colour is an indicator of temperature**. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: The colour of an opaque object is due to the colour it
questions.questionText: The colour of an opaque object is due to the colour it
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **White light** contains all visible wavelengths. An opaque object **absorbs some wavelengths** and **reflects others**.
The perceived colour is the **wavelength(s) reflected into our eyes**. Thus a red apple appears red because it **reflects red** and absorbs others.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: The acceleration due to gravity of a catastrophic earthquake will be
questions.questionText: The acceleration due to gravity of a catastrophic earthquake will be
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Standard gravity g 980 cm/s**. In a very powerful earthquake, **ground acceleration can momentarily exceed g**, throwing objects into the air.
Therefore, **acceleration > 980 cm/s is possible** in catastrophic events. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: The speed of light will be minimum while passing through
questions.questionText: The speed of light will be minimum while passing through
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Speed of light** in a medium is **c/n**, where n is the refractive index. **Higher n ' lower speed**.
Approximate refractive indices: **vacuum = 1.0, air 1.0003, water 1.33, glass 1.5**. **Glass has the highest refractive index** among the options, so light **travels slowest in glass**.
Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: The half-life of a radioactive element is 5 years. The fraction of the radioactive substance that remains after 20 years is
questions.questionText: The half-life of a radioactive element is 5 years. The fraction of the radioactive substance that remains after 20 years is
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Half-life** is the time for half the radioactive atoms to decay. **Number of half-lives in 20 years = 20 / 5 = 4**.
**Remaining fraction = (1/2) = 1/16**. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: A corked bottle full of water when frozen will break because
questions.questionText: A corked bottle full of water when frozen will break because

options.b: The volume of water decreases on freezing
options.c: The volume of water increases on freezing
options.d: Glass is a bad conductor of heat

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. This involves the **anomalous expansion of water**. Unlike most substances, **water expands when it freezes** because ice has a crystalline lattice that occupies more volume.
The **increase in volume exerts pressure** on the sealed bottle, which can **break the glass**. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Which one of the following combinations of aperture and shutter speed of a camera will allow the maximum exposure?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following combinations of aperture and shutter speed of a camera will allow the maximum exposure?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Exposure** = amount of light reaching the sensor, determined by **aperture and shutter speed**.
**Aperture**: larger f-number = **smaller opening** (less light). **Shutter speed**: **longer duration** = more light. The given combinations are reciprocal equivalents (they balance aperture and shutter speed), but the **longest duration** among options is **1/60 s** (a).
Therefore, option (a) has the **longest exposure time** and thus allows maximum exposure under the interpretation of longest duration. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: If a gas is compressed to half of its original volume at 27C, to what temperature should it be heated to make it occupy its original volume?
questions.questionText: If a gas is compressed to half of its original volume at 27C, to what temperature should it be heated to make it occupy its original volume?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. This problem uses **Charles's Law: V/T = V/T**, with temperatures in Kelvin. Initial temperature **T = 27C = 300 K**. If the gas is compressed to **V = V/2** (at 300 K) and we want **V = V**, then: **(V/2)/300 = V/T** ' **T = 300 x 2 = 600K**.
Convert back to Celsius: **T (C) = 600 K-273 = 327C**. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: One mole of hydrogen gas burns in excess of oxygen to give 290 kJ of heat. What is the amount of heat produced when 4g of hydrogen gas is burnt under the same conditions?
questions.questionText: One mole of hydrogen gas burns in excess of oxygen to give 290 kJ of heat. What is the amount of heat produced when 4g of hydrogen gas is burnt under the same conditions?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. First, relate the mass of hydrogen to moles. The **molar mass of hydrogen gas (H)** is approximately **2 g/mol**.
Burning **one mole of H produces 290 kJ** of heat. Since one mole of H is 2 g, burning 2 g of H produces 290 kJ. **4 g of hydrogen gas is 2 moles** (4 g / 2 g/mol = 2 moles).
**Total heat produced = 2 moles-290 kJ/mole = 580 kJ**. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: A man jumped at a speed of 5 metres per second from a stationary boat and the boat moved off with the speed of 0.5 metre per second. How many times is the mass of the boat greater than that of the man?
questions.questionText: A man jumped at a speed of 5 metres per second from a stationary boat and the boat moved off with the speed of 0.5 metre per second. How many times is the mass of the boat greater than that of the man?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. This problem is an application of the law of **conservation of linear momentum**. The **total momentum** of the system (man + boat) **before the jump is zero**, as they are stationary. Therefore, the total momentum **after the jump must also be zero**.
Let m be the mass of the man and v be his velocity (5 m/s). Let m be the mass of the boat and v be its velocity (
- 0.5 m/s, the negative sign indicates it moves in the opposite direction).
According to conservation of momentum: **mv + mv = 0** Substituting the values: **(m-5) + (m - - 0.5) = 0** ' **5m = 0.5m**
Therefore **m / m = 5 / 0.5 = 10**. The **mass of the boat is 10 times greater** than the mass of the man. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: In an electronic watch, the component corresponding to the pendulum of a pendulum clock is a
questions.questionText: In an electronic watch, the component corresponding to the pendulum of a pendulum clock is a
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. The function of a pendulum in a pendulum clock is to act as a **timekeeping element** by oscillating at a very regular and predictable frequency.
In a modern electronic watch (specifically a quartz watch), this timekeeping function is performed by a **crystal oscillator**. A tiny, tuning-fork-shaped quartz crystal is made to **vibrate at an extremely precise frequency** (usually 32,768 Hz) by an electric current from the battery.
An integrated circuit counts these vibrations to **keep accurate time**. A balance wheel (d) is the timekeeping element in a mechanical watch, not an electronic one.
Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Consider the figure of a fountain with four holes given below: Which one of the holes in the fountain will throw the water farthest?
questions.questionText: Consider the figure of a fountain with four holes given below: Which one of the holes in the fountain will throw the water farthest?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. This problem relates to **Torricelli's law**, which describes the speed of fluid flowing out of an orifice.
The speed of the water exiting a hole is given by **v = (2gh)**, where g is the acceleration due to gravity and h is the depth of the hole below the water's surface.
The horizontal distance (range) the water travels is given by **Range = v-t**, where t is the time it takes to fall to the ground. The time of fall depends on the **height of the hole above the ground**. The **maximum range** is achieved from a hole located at the **midpoint of the total water height**.
In the given diagram, **hole 2 is located closest to the vertical midpoint** of the water column. Water from this hole will have an **optimal combination of horizontal exit speed and time in the air**, allowing it to travel the farthest horizontal distance.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: The pitch of the voice of women is in general
questions.questionText: The pitch of the voice of women is in general

options.a: Marginally lower than that of men
options.c: Much lower than that of men
options.d: The same as that of men

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Pitch** in sound is determined by the **frequency of the sound waves**. A higher frequency corresponds to a **higher pitch**.
In humans, the frequency of the voice is determined by the **vibration of the vocal cords**. Women generally have **shorter and thinner vocal cords** than men.
This anatomical difference causes their vocal cords to **vibrate at a higher frequency**. As a result, the **pitch of a woman's voice is typically higher** than that of a man.
Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: At what temperature are the readings of a Centigrade and Fahrenheit thermometer the same?
questions.questionText: At what temperature are the readings of a Centigrade and Fahrenheit thermometer the same?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. The conversion formula between Celsius (C) and Fahrenheit (F) is: **F = (9/5)C + 32**. For equal readings, set **F = C = T**. **T = (9/5)T + 32 T - (9/5)T = 32**
**( - 4/5)T = 32**
**T = - 40**.
Therefore, at **- 40**, both thermometers show the same reading.
Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: The formation of brilliant colours in a soap film is a consequence of the phenomenon of
questions.questionText: The formation of brilliant colours in a soap film is a consequence of the phenomenon of
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. The brilliant colours seen in a soap film or oil film result from **thin-film interference**.
Light waves **reflect from both the top and bottom surfaces** of the film. These two reflected waves **interfere with each other**. **Constructive interference** enhances certain colours. **Destructive interference** cancels others. This process involves **multiple reflections** inside the thin film and the resulting **interference pattern**.
Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Which one of the following is NOT an astronomical object?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is NOT an astronomical object?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Pulsar**: A highly magnetized, rotating neutron star emitting electromagnetic radiation " an **astronomical object**.
**Brittle Star**: A **marine animal**, belonging to the echinoderm family (related to starfish), found on the sea floor " **not an astronomical object**.
**Black Hole**: A region of spacetime with gravitational pull so strong that nothing, not even light, can escape " **astronomical object**. **Quasar**: An extremely luminous and distant active galactic nucleus, powered by a supermassive black hole " **astronomical object**.
Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Consider the figures of a metal ball and a metal ring given below: The metal ball can just pass through the hole of a metal ring formed out of a strip. When the ball is heated it gets stuck. But when the metal ring is heated,
questions.questionText: Consider the figures of a metal ball and a metal ring given below: The metal ball can just pass through the hole of a metal ring formed out of a strip. When the ball is heated it gets stuck. But when the metal ring is heated,

options.a: The ball can still pass through it because the ring diameter expands on heating
options.b: The ball gets stuck because the diameter of the hole decreases on expansion
options.c: The ball will still pass through because the hole diameter does not change
options.d: The ball will pass through because there is no change in the ring

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. This demonstrates the principle of **thermal expansion** " most materials expand when heated.
When the metal ring is heated, it **expands in all directions** " including the circumference of the hole. A common misconception is that the hole shrinks, but in reality, the **entire ring expands, and the hole gets larger**.
Think of it as a photographic enlargement " **every part, including the hole, increases in size**. Thus, when the ring is heated, the **hole enlarges**, allowing the ball to pass through easily.
Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: The working of a quartz crystal in the watch is based on the
questions.questionText: The working of a quartz crystal in the watch is based on the
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. The timekeeping mechanism in a quartz watch relies on the **piezoelectric effect**. The piezoelectric effect is the ability of certain crystals (like quartz) to **generate a small electric voltage** when subjected to **mechanical stress**.
Conversely, when electric voltage is applied, they **vibrate mechanically**. In a quartz watch, a small battery sends current to a quartz crystal, causing it to **vibrate precisely 32,768 times per second**.
An electronic circuit counts these vibrations and converts them into **one-second pulses** to drive the watch hands or display. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Greenhouse Gases (GHG), ODS, Related Terms, Global Warming
statementLines: When there is depletion of ozone in the stratosphere, the wavelength of radiation striking the earth's surface will be
questions.questionText: When there is depletion of ozone in the stratosphere, the wavelength of radiation striking the earth's surface will be
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. The ozone layer primarily absorbs **ultraviolet (UV) radiation** from the Sun. UV radiation occupies the portion of the electromagnetic spectrum with wavelengths between **10 nm and 400 nm** (10 m to 4 x 10 m).
When the ozone layer is depleted, more harmful **UVB and UVC radiation** reach the Earth's surface. Among the given options, **10 m (100 nm)** lies in the **UV range**.
The other options correspond to X-rays (10 m), microwaves/infrared (10 m), and radio waves (10 m) " none of which are relevant to ozone depletion.
Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: A body is attached to a spring balance suspended from a stand. The reading on the balance is 0.5 kg. The two together are detached from the stand and allowed to fall through a height. While falling, the reading in the balance will be
questions.questionText: A body is attached to a spring balance suspended from a stand. The reading on the balance is 0.5 kg. The two together are detached from the stand and allowed to fall through a height. While falling, the reading in the balance will be

options.b: Less than 0.5 kg but not zero
options.c: More than 0.5 kg depending on the height

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. A spring balance measures **weight through the tension** in the spring. The reading of 0.5 kg corresponds to the weight of the body when **stationary**.
When the system is in **free fall**, both the body and the balance **accelerate downward at the same rate (g)**. The body exerts **no relative force** on the spring, creating a condition of **weightlessness**.
Therefore, the spring is **unstretched**, and the balance reads **zero**. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Which one of the following will take place when a watch based on an oscillating spring is taken to a deep mine?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following will take place when a watch based on an oscillating spring is taken to a deep mine?

options.c: It will indicate the same time as on earth's surface

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. A spring-based watch operates using an **oscillating balance spring system**. The time period depends on the **spring constant (k)** and **moment of inertia (I)** of the balance wheel. **T = 2(I/k)** This motion **does not depend on gravity (g)**.
Even though gravity is slightly less in a deep mine, it has **no effect on the working** of a spring-based watch. A pendulum clock, however, would become faster or slower, since it depends on gravity. Therefore, the spring-based watch will **indicate the same time**.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Which one of the following figures represents the correct path of a ray of light through a glass prism?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following figures represents the correct path of a ray of light through a glass prism?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. When light passes through a prism, it undergoes **refraction twice**. **First refraction**: As light enters the denser medium (glass) from air, it **bends towards the normal**.
**Second refraction**: As light exits from glass to air, it **bends away from the normal**. The net deviation is **towards the base of the prism**.
Figure (c) correctly shows the ray bending towards the normal on entry and away from the normal on exit. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Match List I with List II and
select the correct answer by using the codes given below the lists:
1. A. Barometer -
1. Used for measuring altitudes and angles in navigation and astronomy

4. D. Quadrant -

questions.questionText: Match List I with List II and select the correct answer by using the codes given below the lists: 1. A. Barometer - 1. Used for measuring altitudes and angles in navigation and astronomy 2. B. Chronometer - 2. Used for measurement of angular distances between two objects 3. C. Sextant - 3. Keeps very accurate time as the one that is used to determine longitude at sea 4. D. Quadrant - 4. Used for measuring atmospheric pressure 5. 5. An instrument used in the diagnosis of pregnancy Codes:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Barometer** " measures **atmospheric pressure** ' (4) **Chronometer** " a highly accurate timepiece used to **determine longitude at sea** ' (3) **Sextant** " measures **angular distances between two objects** ' (2) **Quadrant**
- measures **altitudes and angles** in navigation and astronomy ' (1)
Correct matching: **A"4, B"3, C"2, D"1**. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: What is the increasing order of the wavelengths of the following colours?
1. Orange
2. Indigo
3. Yellow
4. Violet
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.questionText: What is the increasing order of the wavelengths of the following colours? 1. Orange 2. Indigo 3. Yellow 4. Violet Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. The colors of the visible spectrum follow the order **VIBGYOR** (Violet ' Indigo ' Blue ' Green ' Yellow ' Orange ' Red).
This is also the order of **increasing wavelength**. Hence, starting with the **shortest to longest wavelength**: Violet (4) Indigo (2) Yellow (3) Orange (1) Therefore, the correct sequence is **4, 2, 3, 1**.
Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: A wooden block is floating in water. If 4/5 of its volume is immersed in water and the volume of the water displaced is 800 ml, the volume of the block is
questions.questionText: A wooden block is floating in water. If 4/5 of its volume is immersed in water and the volume of the water displaced is 800 ml, the volume of the block is
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. According to **Archimedes' principle**, the volume of a floating object that is submerged is **equal to the volume of the fluid it displaces**.
The problem states that the volume of water displaced is **800 ml**. This means the **volume of the block immersed** in water is also 800 ml.
Given that this immersed volume (800 ml) is **4/5 of the total volume**, let total volume = V. Then, **(4/5) - V = 800 ml** ' **V = 800 - (5/4) = 1000 ml**.
Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: The rainbow and the rainbow-like colours seen on a thin film of oil on a water surface are two different phenomena. They are
questions.questionText: The rainbow and the rainbow-like colours seen on a thin film of oil on a water surface are two different phenomena. They are

options.a: Similar in nature and both are due to refraction of light
options.b: Different in nature and rainbow is due to interference of light, while colour of thin film is due to diffraction
options.c: Similar in nature and both are due to interference of light
options.d: Different in nature and rainbow is due to refraction, while colour of thin film is due to interference of light

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. The two phenomena are indeed different and caused by **different optical principles**. A **rainbow** is formed by the **dispersion and total internal reflection** of sunlight by water droplets in the atmosphere.
As light enters a droplet, it **refracts**, reflects, and disperses into its constituent colors. The iridescent colours seen on an **oil film** are caused by **thin-film interference**.
Light waves reflecting off the top and bottom surfaces of the film **interfere constructively or destructively** depending on film thickness and angle of viewing.
Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: A cubic metre of water at 0 C is solidified into ice. Density of ice is 0.96 of water at 0
C. Which of the following deductions are true?
1. Water expands when solidified.
2. Ice will float in water at 0 C with half its volume above the surface.
3. Density of all liquids are higher than its density when solidified.
Select the correct answer from the codes given below:
questions.questionText: A cubic metre of water at 0 C is solidified into ice. Density of ice is 0.96 of water at 0 C. Which of the following deductions are true? 1. Water expands when solidified. 2. Ice will float in water at 0 C with half its volume above the surface. 3. Density of all liquids are higher than its density when solidified. Select the correct answer from the codes given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. Statement 1 is true. The **density of ice** (0.96 g/cm) is **less than the density of water** (1.0 g/cm). Since **Density = Mass/Volume**, for a constant mass, a lower density implies a **larger volume**.
Therefore, **water expands when it solidifies** into ice. Statement 2 is false. The fraction submerged is equal to density ratio (/ = 0.96/1.0 = 0.96), meaning **96% submerged, 4% above**, not half.
Statement 3 is false. This is a generalization that is not true. **Water is an exception**; most substances are denser when solid. Therefore, only

Statement 1 is correct. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Weights of two pieces, one of gold and one of silver each weighing 100 gm were recorded on a spring balance. Then they were fully immersed in a liquid bath, by letting them hang through a light thread tied to the balance. Compare the recorded weights of the two pieces under that condition. Then
questions.questionText: Weights of two pieces, one of gold and one of silver each weighing 100 gm were recorded on a spring balance. Then they were fully immersed in a liquid bath, by letting them hang through a light thread tied to the balance. Compare the recorded weights of the two pieces under that condition. Then

options.a: The gold piece will weigh more than the silver piece
options.b: The silver piece will weigh more than the gold piece
options.c: The weights of the two pieces will be equal
options.d: Nothing can be said without knowing the density of the liquid

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. When an object is immersed in a liquid, it experiences an **upward buoyant force** equal to the weight of the liquid displaced (**Archimedes' principle**).
The apparent weight recorded by the spring balance will be: **Apparent Weight = True Weight-Buoyant Force**. Both pieces have the **same true weight** (100 gm). The density of gold (19.3 g/cm) is **much higher** than the density of silver (10.5 g/cm).
For the same mass (100 gm), the **silver piece will have a larger volume** than the gold piece. Because the silver piece has a larger volume, it will **displace more liquid** and therefore experience a **greater buoyant force**.
Since the silver piece has a larger buoyant force acting on it, its apparent weight will be **reduced more** than the gold piece's apparent weight.
Therefore, when immersed, the **gold piece will weigh more** than the silver piece. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: In solar heaters, in which adequate sunlight can be used to provide safe drinking water in far-off areas, the water to be purified is contained in a black tray. The lid of the tray is made of glass. Water gets converted into steam/vapours, which condense on the glass, slides to the sides/edges of the glass plates and drops into another container and is collected. The most appropriate shape of the glass lid could be
questions.questionText: In solar heaters, in which adequate sunlight can be used to provide safe drinking water in far-off areas, the water to be purified is contained in a black tray. The lid of the tray is made of glass. Water gets converted into steam/vapours, which condense on the glass, slides to the sides/edges of the glass plates and drops into another container and is collected. The most appropriate shape of the glass lid could be
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. The device described is a **solar still**, which uses solar energy to purify water through **distillation**.
Sunlight heats the water in the black tray, causing it to evaporate. The water vapor rises and comes into contact with the **cooler inner surface of the glass lid**.
The vapor condenses back into liquid water droplets on the glass. For the collected pure water to be channeled into a separate container, the **glass lid must be sloped**.
The shape in (c), a **tilted flat plane or a simple A-frame**, is the most effective design. The condensed droplets will run down the sloped surface due to **gravity** and can be collected in a channel at the lower edge.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Superconductors are so called because they
questions.questionText: Superconductors are so called because they

options.a: Efficiently conduct heat from one hot body to another
options.b: Offer no resistance to the conduction of heat at high temperatures
options.c: Offer no resistance to the conduction of electricity at low temperatures
options.d: Produce high temperatures while conducting electricity

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Superconductivity** is a phenomenon observed in certain materials where their **electrical resistance drops to exactly zero** when they are cooled below a specific critical temperature.
This means they can conduct electricity with **perfect efficiency**, without any energy loss as heat. This property of offering **no resistance to the conduction of electricity** is what defines a superconductor.
This phenomenon occurs only at **very low temperatures** (though research into high-temperature superconductors is ongoing). Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: An instrument measures the voltage of a 50 Hz sinusoidal current as 220
V. The mean average voltage of the sinusoidal current is
questions.questionText: An instrument measures the voltage of a 50 Hz sinusoidal current as 220 V. The mean average voltage of the sinusoidal current is
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. A **sinusoidal current (or voltage) is an alternating current (AC)** that varies sinusoidally over time.
It has a **positive half-cycle and a negative half-cycle that are perfectly symmetrical**. The mean average voltage over one complete cycle is calculated by integrating the voltage over the cycle and dividing by the period.
Because the **positive and negative half-cycles are identical in shape but opposite in sign, they cancel each other out perfectly** over a full cycle.
Therefore, the **mean average voltage of any pure sinusoidal AC waveform is always zero**. The **220V measurement is the RMS (Root Mean Square) voltage, not the average voltage**.
Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: The path of Halley's comet in its orbit around the sun is
questions.questionText: The path of Halley's comet in its orbit around the sun is
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. Comets that are gravitationally bound to the Sun and return periodically travel in **elliptical orbits**.
**Halley's Comet is a famous periodic comet with a highly elliptical orbit**. Its orbit takes it very close to the Sun (perihelion) and then far out into the outer solar system (aphelion).
Circular orbits are a special case of an ellipse. Parabolic and hyperbolic paths are associated with objects that make a single pass by the Sun and then escape its gravity, never to return.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: A passing motorcycle causes distortion or disturbance with the reception of a signal in radio and T.
V. The cause of this is
questions.questionText: A passing motorcycle causes distortion or disturbance with the reception of a signal in radio and T. V. The cause of this is

options.a: The intense sound waves affect the small electronic devices of the receptor
options.b: The vehicle's metal parts block/distort the radio waves
options.c: The spark plug fitted in the engine produces electro-magnetic signals due to sparking
options.d: Modern motorcycles are fitted with an electric ignition system which produces radio waves

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. The interference experienced by radio and TV signals from a passing motorcycle (with a gasoline engine) is a form of **electromagnetic interference (EMI)**.
The **spark plug in a gasoline engine creates a high-voltage electrical spark** to ignite the fuel-air mixture. This rapid, **high-energy electrical discharge generates a burst of broad-spectrum electromagnetic radiation (radio waves)**.
These **unwanted radio waves are picked up by the radio or TV antenna** and are interpreted as static or noise, causing distortion in the reception.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Assume that " A " denotes narrow wheel with wider wheel.
B " denotes small free wheel with large wheel.
C " denotes gear wheel with large wider wheel.
In a rural area where there are sandy tracts, it will be advisable to use a bicycle with
questions.questionText: Assume that " A " denotes narrow wheel with wider wheel. B " denotes small free wheel with large wheel. C " denotes gear wheel with large wider wheel. In a rural area where there are sandy tracts, it will be advisable to use a bicycle with
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. This question is about choosing the best bicycle setup for riding on sandy terrain. **Sandy tracts are soft surfaces**. To avoid sinking into the sand, the **pressure exerted on the ground needs to be minimized**.
**Pressure = Force / Area**. A **wider wheel (or tire) has a larger contact area with the ground**. For the same force (the weight of the rider and bicycle), a **larger area results in lower pressure**.
This **prevents the wheel from digging into the sand**. Therefore, using a bicycle with wider wheels is most advisable for sandy tracts. Option (a) describes this choice.
Gears (C) help with pedaling effort but don't address the sinking issue. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Photons have properties similar to that of
questions.questionText: Photons have properties similar to that of
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. In modern physics, the concept of **waveparticle duality is fundamental**. It states that all quantum entities, including photons, **exhibit the properties of both particles and waves**.
**Particle Properties**: Photons can be detected as discrete packets of energy, they have momentum, and they can collide with other particles (like in the photoelectric effect).
**Wave Properties**: Photons also exhibit behaviors characteristic of waves, such as diffraction (bending around obstacles) and interference (creating patterns of reinforcement and cancellation when they overlap).
Therefore, a photon is not strictly a particle or a wave, but **has properties of both particles and waves**. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Given below are colours of the visible spectrum. Identify the correct sequence in order of increasing wavelengths:
questions.questionText: Given below are colours of the visible spectrum. Identify the correct sequence in order of increasing wavelengths:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. In the visible spectrum, colours are arranged in order of **increasing wavelength as follows: Violet ' Blue ' Green ' Yellow ' Orange ' Red**.
**Violet has the shortest wavelength (390" 455 nm) and Red the longest (622"780 nm)**. Therefore, in the given options, the correct **increasing wavelength order is Violet, Blue, Yellow, Red**.
Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Assertion (A): When a wheel is in motion, a point (on the rim) which is farthest away from the ground will have the highest speed while the point (on the rim) closest to the ground will have the lowest speed.
Reason (R): The speed of the point on the rim in the direction of motion and along the circumference of the wheel are additive.
questions.questionText: Assertion (A): When a wheel is in motion, a point (on the rim) which is farthest away from the ground will have the highest speed while the point (on the rim) closest to the ground will have the lowest speed. Reason (R): The speed of the point on the rim in the direction of motion and along the circumference of the wheel are additive.

options.a: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
options.b: Both A and R are true but R is not a correct explanation of A.
options.c: A is true but R is false.
options.d: A is false but R is tru

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. The **topmost point on a rotating wheel moves forward with both the translational speed of the wheel and the rotational speed** of that point " hence, **speeds are additive**.
The **bottom point is momentarily at rest relative to the ground** because its rotational motion cancels the translational motion. Thus, the **top point moves fastest, and the bottom point slowest**, exactly as explained by the reason. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Assertion (A): When a ship moves from the sea water into fresh water of a river, the ship will relatively sink.
Reason (R): The density of sea water is higher than that of the fresh water.
questions.questionText: Assertion (A): When a ship moves from the sea water into fresh water of a river, the ship will relatively sink. Reason (R): The density of sea water is higher than that of the fresh water.

options.a: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
options.b: Both A and R are true but R is not a correct explanation of A.
options.c: A is true but R is false.
options.d: A is false but R is tru

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Seawater has a higher density** due to the presence of dissolved salts. **Fresh water is less dense**, so to displace the same weight of water, the **ship must sink deeper when it moves into fresh water**.
Therefore, **both the assertion and reason are true, and the reason correctly explains the assertion**. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Assertion (A): A tennis ball will bounce higher in Calcutta than it would in Shimla.
Reason (R): The downward pull due to gravity is less and air is more rarefied in Shimla than in Calcutta.
questions.questionText: Assertion (A): A tennis ball will bounce higher in Calcutta than it would in Shimla. Reason (R): The downward pull due to gravity is less and air is more rarefied in Shimla than in Calcutta.

options.a: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
options.b: Both A and R are true but R is not a correct explanation of A.
options.c: A is true but R is false.
options.d: A is false but R is tru

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. The **force of gravity is slightly less at higher altitudes** because they are farther from the Earths center.
**Shimla, being at a higher altitude than Calcutta, has weaker gravity**. Therefore, **a tennis ball would bounce higher in Shimla**, not in Calcutta.
The **assertion is false, but the reason (less gravity and rarer air at higher altitude) is true**. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: The evidence for the rotation of stars comes mainly from the
questions.questionText: The evidence for the rotation of stars comes mainly from the
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. The **Doppler Effect refers to the change in frequency or wavelength of waves** (sound or light) due to the relative motion between the source and observer.
In astronomy, the **red shift (movement away) and blue shift (movement toward) of light from stars provide evidence of their rotation and motion**.
The expanding universe theory and details about galactic rotation are derived from Doppler shift observations. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: The conversion of electrical energy into chemical energy is observed in
questions.questionText: The conversion of electrical energy into chemical energy is observed in
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. A fan converts electrical energy into mechanical energy. A heater converts electrical energy into thermal energy.
An incandescent bulb converts electrical energy into heat and light. A **storage battery, on the other hand, converts electrical energy into chemical energy** through oxidation at the anode and reduction at the cathode.
Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Birds get thrust (forward motion) and lift (upward motion) from the
questions.questionText: Birds get thrust (forward motion) and lift (upward motion) from the

options.c: Shape of wings which is similar to aeroplane blades

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. Lift is largely generated by the airfoil shape of the wings (pressure difference top vs bottom), while **thrust is produced mainly by the flapping motion**.
The **down-and-back stroke of flapping pushes air backward, producing forward thrust** (Newtons Third Law), and also contributes to additional lift.
While wing shape matters for lift, the **flapping provides both primary thrust and dynamic lift needed in active flight**. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: A small weight put on the pressure cooker increases the pressure of steam because
questions.questionText: A small weight put on the pressure cooker increases the pressure of steam because

options.b: Area of contact is very small
options.c: Density of metal is very high
options.d: The weight acts as a valve, trapping steam until a specific pressure is reached.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. The **small weight sits on the cookers vent and functions as a pressure-regulating valve**. As steam builds, the weight traps it, **allowing pressure to increase until the steam force lifts the weight** and a pulse of steam escapes.
This mechanism maintains a constant elevated pressure and temperature for efficient cooking. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Pressure, Winds, Airmasses (Coriolis Force, Frictional Force, etc.)
statementLines: Atmospheric pressure exerted on earth is due to the
questions.questionText: Atmospheric pressure exerted on earth is due to the
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Atmospheric pressure is the force per unit area due to the weight of the air column above a surface**.
The **Earths gravity holds the atmosphere** and causes the air mass above to exert pressure on the surface. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: 'Black holes' refers to
questions.questionText: 'Black holes' refers to
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. A **black hole is the result of a massive star's gravitational collapse, producing an object of extremely high density**.
Its gravity is so strong that **nothing, not even light, can escape from within its event horizon**. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Heat from the sun is received by the earth through
questions.questionText: Heat from the sun is received by the earth through
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. The space between the Sun and Earth is essentially a vacuum, so **conduction and convection cannot transfer heat across it**.
Energy travels as **electromagnetic radiation (visible light, infrared, UV)**, which does not require a medium. Thus the **Earth receives the Suns heat by radiation**.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Which of the following is true?
questions.questionText: Which of the following is true?

options.c: Both the light and sound waves exhibit interference.
options.d: Neither sound waves nor light waves exhibit interferenc

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Interference is a fundamental property of waves**"it occurs when two or more waves superpose.
**Both sound and light are wave phenomena and therefore exhibit interference**, giving rise to constructive and destructive patterns. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: Insolation refers to
questions.questionText: Insolation refers to

options.a: Restraining the flow of electric current
options.c: Exposure to the rays of the sun

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Insolation = incoming solar radiation**; it denotes the amount of solar energy reaching a surface.
It is a key meteorological/climatological term that quantifies **exposure to the Sun's rays**. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Very small-time intervals are accurately measured by the
questions.questionText: Very small-time intervals are accurately measured by the
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Atomic clocks use the resonant frequency of atoms (e.g., caesium-133)** as their timekeeping standard.
The atomic oscillations are extremely regular, allowing **measurement of very small time intervals with outstanding precision**. Quartz clocks are accurate but less precise than atomic clocks; pulsars are astronomical timekeepers but not standard instruments for general use.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: A chair is tilted about two of its legs and then left. It would return to its original position if
questions.questionText: A chair is tilted about two of its legs and then left. It would return to its original position if

options.a: Its centre of gravity falls outside the base.
options.b: Its centre of gravity falls within the base.
options.c: It is tilted through an angle of 60.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Stability depends on the position of the centre of gravity (CG)** relative to the base of support.
**If the vertical line from the CG falls within the base, the object is stable** and will return to its original position. If the CG moves outside the base, the object becomes unstable and will topple. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Total internal reflection occurs when light travels from
questions.questionText: Total internal reflection occurs when light travels from

options.a: A rarer medium to a denser medium
options.b: A denser medium to a rarer medium
options.c: A rarer medium to a denser medium and the angle of incidence is less than the critical angle.
options.d: A rarer medium to a denser medium and the angle of incidence is greater than the critical angl

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Total Internal Reflection (TIR) occurs when light tries to pass from a denser medium to a rarer medium** (e.g., glass ' air).
A **second necessary condition is that the angle of incidence exceeds the critical angle** for that interface. Option (b) correctly identifies the required direction of travel for TIR to be possible.
Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: A boat filled with some stones is floating in water. If the stones are dropped into the water, the level of the water will
questions.questionText: A boat filled with some stones is floating in water. If the stones are dropped into the water, the level of the water will
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. This is a classic physics problem involving **Archimedes' Principle**. **Case 1 (stones in the boat):** the floating boat displaces a volume of water whose weight equals the combined weight of the boat and stones.
**Case 2 (stones in the water):** the stones displace water equal to their own volume, and the boat now displaces less water. Because **stones are much denser than water**, the submerged volume they occupy is much less than the volume they caused the boat to displace while onboard.
Therefore the **total displaced volume (and thus the water level) decreases** when the stones are dropped overboard. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: The limit beyond which stars suffer internal collapse is called
questions.questionText: The limit beyond which stars suffer internal collapse is called
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. The **Chandrasekhar limit (~1.4 solar masses) is the maximum mass a white dwarf can have** while being supported by electron degeneracy pressure.
If a white dwarf exceeds this limit, **electron degeneracy pressure fails and the star can collapse**, possibly leading to a supernova or formation of a neutron star.
The Eddington limit concerns maximum luminosity before radiation pressure overcomes gravity. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: The phenomenon of mirage is due to
questions.questionText: The phenomenon of mirage is due to

options.a: Increased absorption of light by air at higher temperature
options.b: Change in the refractive index of air with the change in temperature
options.d: Decreased absorption of light by air at higher temperature

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. A mirage occurs because **layers of air at different temperatures have different densities, thus different refractive indices**.
**Light rays bend (refract) when passing through these layers**; on hot surfaces the refractive index gradient can bend rays upwards, making distant sky or objects appear as reflections on the ground.
While total internal reflection can sometimes be involved, the **fundamental cause is the change in refractive index with temperature**. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: On the Moon, an astronaut inflates a rubber balloon with hydrogen gas and releases it at a height of 2 metres. The balloon will
questions.questionText: On the Moon, an astronaut inflates a rubber balloon with hydrogen gas and releases it at a height of 2 metres. The balloon will

options.c: Remain at the height of 2 metres
options.d: First go up and finally come down

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Buoyancy requires a surrounding fluid (atmosphere)**; an object rises if it is less dense than the fluid it displaces.
The **Moon has no significant atmosphere (essentially a vacuum)**, so **no buoyant force will act on the balloon**. With no air to displace, the **only significant force is the Moons gravity, so the balloon will fall down**.
Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: A free-floating astronaut 'A' pushes another free-floating astronaut 'B' in space. The mass of 'A' is greater than that of 'B'. The force exerted by the astronaut 'A' on the astronaut 'B' will be
questions.questionText: A free-floating astronaut 'A' pushes another free-floating astronaut 'B' in space. The mass of 'A' is greater than that of 'B'. The force exerted by the astronaut 'A' on the astronaut 'B' will be

options.b: Equal to the force exerted by 'B' on 'A'.
options.c: Greater than the force exerted by 'B' on 'A'.
options.d: Less than the force exerted by 'B' on 'A'.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. By **Newton's Third Law, every action has an equal and opposite reaction**. When **A pushes B, B pushes A with an equal magnitude force in the opposite direction**. The masses affect the resulting accelerations (F = ma) " the less massive astronaut will experience greater acceleration " but **the forces are equal and opposite**.
Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: Seawater is saltier than rainwater because
questions.questionText: Seawater is saltier than rainwater because

options.b: Rivers wash away salts from the earth and pour them into the sea.
options.c: The air around the sea is saltish.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Rain erodes rocks on land, dissolving minerals and salts**. **Rivers and streams carry these dissolved salts into the oceans**.
When water evaporates from the ocean, the **salts remain behind, causing gradual accumulation of salt** over geological time. This continuous cycle makes the seawater salty, whereas rainwater is relatively pure. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: If camera A has an f/4.5 lens and camera B has an f/2.8 lens, and the diameters of both the lenses are equal, then
questions.questionText: If camera A has an f/4.5 lens and camera B has an f/2.8 lens, and the diameters of both the lenses are equal, then

options.a: Pictures taken by A will always be sharper.
options.b: Pictures taken by B will always be sharper.
options.c: A is better for photographing fast-moving objects.
options.d: B is better for photographing fast-moving objects.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. The **f-number (e.g., f/4.5, f/2.8) is the ratio of focal length to aperture diameter; a lower f-number = wider aperture**.
A wider aperture (lower f-number) **allows more light, enabling faster shutter speeds** for the same exposure. **Faster shutter speeds help freeze motion**, so a camera with **f/2.8 (B) is better for photographing fast-moving objects**.
Sharpness depends on many factors (lens quality, depth of field), not just f-number. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: The Sri Lanka Broadcasting Corporation broadcasts its programmes on the 25-metre band.
Which one of the following is the frequency of this transmission?
questions.questionText: The Sri Lanka Broadcasting Corporation broadcasts its programmes on the 25-metre band. Which one of the following is the frequency of this transmission?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Wave speed relation: c = f -**, where **c 3-10 m/s** and **= 25 m**.
Frequency: **f = c / = (3-10) / 25 = 1.2-10 Hz = 12-10 Hz = 12 MHz**. Therefore the 25-metre band corresponds to **~12 Mega Hertz (MHz)**. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: The resolving power of an electron microscope is much higher than that of an ordinary light microscope because resolving power increases
questions.questionText: The resolving power of an electron microscope is much higher than that of an ordinary light microscope because resolving power increases

options.a: If the wavelength is short and the wavelength of the electron beam can be made quite small by accelerating the electrons.
options.b: If the wavelength is short and light given by electrons has very short wavelength.
options.c: If magnification is high and electronic lens provides much higher magnification
options.d: If magnification is high and the electron microscope has a battery of lenses to provide high magnification.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Resolving power depends fundamentally on the wavelength of the illuminating radiation " shorter wavelength ' higher resolution**.
A light microscope uses visible light (wavelengths ~400"700 nm). An **electron microscope uses accelerated electrons, whose de Broglie wavelength can be made extremely short by high acceleration** (much shorter than visible light).
That **very short wavelength is the primary reason for the vastly superior resolving power** of electron microscopes. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Let E1, E2, E3 denote the efficiency of a diesel, a petrol, and a steam engine respectively. Which of the following is correct?
questions.questionText: Let E1, E2, E3 denote the efficiency of a diesel, a petrol, and a steam engine respectively. Which of the following is correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Steam engines (E3) are external combustion engines with the lowest efficiency (=15-20%)** due to large heat losses.
**Petrol (gasoline) engines (E2) are internal combustion engines with moderate efficiency (=25-30%)**. **Diesel engines (E1) operate at higher compression ratios and therefore have higher thermal efficiency (=40-45%)**.
Thus the order is **Diesel > Petrol > Steam**, i.e., **E1 > E2 > E3**. Hence, option (c) is correct.
