**Statement 1 is correct: The Shourya missile is a hypersonic surface-to-surface missile capable of speeds above Mach 5, and reports confirm it achieves speeds exceeding Mach 7 or 8 during flight.**

**Statement 2 is correct: The range of the Shourya missile is between 750 km and 1,900 km. Since its upper limit exceeds 1,600 km, the statement is factually correct.** Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Selene-1, the lunar orbiter mission belongs to
which one of the following?
questions.questionText: Selene-1, the lunar orbiter mission belongs to which one of the following?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. SELENE-1 (Selenological and Engineering Explorer), also called Kaguya, was a **Japanese** lunar orbiter mission.
It was developed and launched by the Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA) in September **2007** to study the Moons origin and evolution.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Which one of the following is a spacecraft?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is a spacecraft?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Cassini** was a robotic spacecraft launched jointly by NASA, the European Space Agency (ESA), and the Italian Space Agency (ASI) to study Saturn, its rings, and moons.
Apophis is an asteroid. Spitzer is an infrared space telescope. TechSar is an Israeli reconnaissance satellite. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: In
which one of the following locations is the International Thermonuclear Experimental Reactor (ITER) Project to be built?
questions.questionText: In which one of the following locations is the International Thermonuclear Experimental Reactor (ITER) Project to be built?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. The ITER Project is an international fusion research initiative aimed at demonstrating the feasibility of nuclear fusion.
After global competition, **Southern France** was selected as the project site. The specific location is Cadarache, near Saint-Paul-ls-Durance, in the Provence-Alpes-Cte dAzur region.
Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: What is the purpose of the US Space Agencys Themis Mission, which was recently in the news?
questions.questionText: What is the purpose of the US Space Agencys Themis Mission, which was recently in the news?

options.a: To study the possibility of life on Mars
options.b: To study the satellites of Saturn
options.c: To study the colourful display of high latitude skies
options.d: To build a Space laboratory to study the stellar explosions

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. The THEMIS Mission (Time History of Events and Macroscale Interactions during Substorms) was launched by NASA in **2007**.
Its primary objective was to study the cause of geomagnetic substorms, which lead to the brilliant auroras"the **colourful displays of high-latitude skies** like the Aurora Borealis and Aurora Australis.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: ISRO successfully conducted a rocket test using cryogenic engines in the year
2007. Where is the test-stand used for the purpose, located?
questions.questionText: ISRO successfully conducted a rocket test using cryogenic engines in the year 2007. Where is the test-stand used for the purpose, located?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. A cryogenic engine uses propellants like liquid hydrogen and liquid oxygen stored at extremely low temperatures.
ISROs main facility for testing liquid and cryogenic rocket engines is the ISRO Propulsion Complex (IPRC). This facility is located at **Mahendragiri**, in the Tirunelveli district of Tamil Nadu.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Recently, the European Union and other six countries including India signed the International Thermonuclear Experimental Reactor (ITER) Project.
Which one of the following was not a signatory to it?
questions.questionText: Recently, the European Union and other six countries including India signed the International Thermonuclear Experimental Reactor (ITER) Project. Which one of the following was not a signatory to it?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. ITER is an international scientific megaproject aimed at demonstrating the feasibility of nuclear fusion as a sustainable energy source.
The signatories (**2006**) were: European Union (EU), India, Japan, China, Russia, South Korea, and the United States of America. **Canada** was part of the early planning but withdrew before the agreement was signed. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Which one of the following pairs is not correctly matched?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following pairs is not correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. (a) COBE was a NASA satellite launched in **1989**. (b) Falcon is an under-sea telecommunication cable system.
(c) Discovery was one of NASAs Space Shuttles. (d) Atlantis was also a **Space Shuttle orbiter**, not a Space Station. Mir, Skylab, and ISS are space stations.
Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Which of the following types is used by computed tomography employed for visualization of the internal structure of human body?
questions.questionText: Which of the following types is used by computed tomography employed for visualization of the internal structure of human body?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. Computed Tomography (CT), or CT scan, is an advanced imaging method that provides detailed internal body images.
It uses **X-rays** taken from multiple angles around the body. A computer processes these 2-D X-ray images to produce cross-sectional slices of bones and tissues.
Sound waves are used in Ultrasound, Magnetic resonance in MRI, and Radioisotopes in PET scans. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Consider the following statements in respect of Indias advanced satellite INSAT 4A:
1. INSAT 4A was launched in December, 2005 from New Mexico.
2. The European commercial launch service provider Arianespace was associated with the launch of INSAT 4A.
3. Tata-Sky"a digital cable service provider"is associated with DTH television broadcasting services from INSAT-4A.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements in respect of Indias advanced satellite INSAT 4A: 1. INSAT 4A was launched in December, 2005 from New Mexico. 2. The European commercial launch service provider Arianespace was associated with the launch of INSAT 4A. 3. Tata-Sky"a digital cable service provider"is associated with DTH television broadcasting services from INSAT-4A. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer.

**Statement 1 is incorrect: INSAT-4A was launched on December 22,**2005**, from Kourou, French Guiana, not New Mexico.**

**Statement 2 is correct: The launch was conducted by Arianespace using their Ariane 5 rocket.**

**Statement 3 is correct: Tata Sky (now Tata Play) used the Ku-band transponders on INSAT-4A for its DTH broadcasting services.** Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Which one of the following pairs is not correctly matched?
Recent Scientific Achievement: Country
questions.questionText: Which one of the following pairs is not correctly matched? Recent Scientific Achievement: Country

options.a: Creating the Worlds first mouse: United Kingdom
options.b: Cloning a human embryo for the first time: Germany
options.c: Guiding a spacecraft for Collision with a comet: United States of America
options.d: Landing a spacecraft on an asteroid: Japan

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. Pair (a) is not correctly matched: The first computer mouse was invented by Douglas Engelbart in the United States in the 1960s, not the United Kingdom.
Pair (c) is correctly matched: NASAs Deep Impact spacecraft collided with Comet Tempel 1 in **2005**. Pair (d) is correctly matched: Japans Hayabusa spacecraft landed on the asteroid Itokawa in **2005**.
Pair (b) reflects the strict anti-cloning laws in Germany, though cloning claims came from South Korea at the time. However, (a) is definitively wrong historically.
Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Which one of the following is the landmark performance of Dr.
V. Mohan Reddy, an Indian-born doctor working in USA, during February, 2005?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is the landmark performance of Dr. V. Mohan Reddy, an Indian-born doctor working in USA, during February, 2005?

options.a: Research on human stem cell which is likely to revolutionise treatment of deadly disease like cancer
options.b: Successful open heart procedure called arterial switch performed on an infant
options.c: Research on genetic engineering which can help in treatment of deadly disease like AIDS
options.d: Successful brain surgery to revive the function of nearly dead brain of a premature born infant

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. In February **2005**, Dr. V. Mohan Reddy, a renowned pediatric cardiac surgeon, achieved global recognition for a groundbreaking open-heart surgery.
He successfully performed an arterial switch operation on a premature infant weighing only 1.4 kg, suffering from transposition of the great arteries, a severe congenital heart defect.
The surgery was conducted in Mumbai and was hailed as a landmark medical feat. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. A geostationary satellite is at an approximate height of 10,000 km
2. FM transmission of music is of very good quality because the atmospheric or manmade noises which have generally frequency variations can do little harm.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. A geostationary satellite is at an approximate height of 10,000 km 2. FM transmission of music is of very good quality because the atmospheric or manmade noises which have generally frequency variations can do little harm. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer.

**Statement 1 is incorrect: A geostationary satellite orbits the Earth at a height of approximately 35,786 km (around 36,000 km) above the equator, not 10,000 km.**

**Statement 2 is correct: FM (Frequency Modulation) transmission encodes signals through frequency variations. Most natural and man-made noise causes amplitude variations, so FM receivers reject this noise, resulting in high-quality sound.** Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
(1) Adam Osborne produced the first portable computer (2) Ian Wilmut created the first cloned sheep.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: (1) Adam Osborne produced the first portable computer (2) Ian Wilmut created the first cloned sheep. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. Statement (1) is correct: Adam Osborne founded the Osborne Computer Corporation and introduced the Osborne 1 in **1981**, which became the first commercially successful portable computer.
Statement (2) is correct: Sir Ian Wilmut, a British embryologist at the Roslin Institute, led the team that created Dolly the sheep in **1996**, the first mammal cloned from an adult somatic cell.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: The Genetic Engineering Approval Committee, whose permission is required for cultivation of any genetically modified crop such as Bt-Cotton in India, is under the Union Ministry of:
questions.questionText: The Genetic Engineering Approval Committee, whose permission is required for cultivation of any genetically modified crop such as Bt-Cotton in India, is under the Union Ministry of:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. The Genetic Engineering Appraisal Committee (GEAC) is the apex regulatory authority for genetically modified organisms (GMOs) in India.
It operates under the Environment (Protection) Act, **1986**. Because it operates under this Act, it functions under the **Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC)**, formerly known as the Ministry of Environment and Forests.
Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. India launched its first full-fledged meteorological satellite (METSAT) in September, 2002.
2. For the first time, the space vehicle PSLV-C4 carried a payload of more than 1000 kg into a geosynchronous orbit.
Which of these statements is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. India launched its first full-fledged meteorological satellite (METSAT) in September, 2002. 2. For the first time, the space vehicle PSLV-C4 carried a payload of more than 1000 kg into a geosynchronous orbit. Which of these statements is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer.

**Statement 1 is correct: On September 12,**2002**, ISRO launched METSAT, Indias first dedicated meteorological satellite, marking a significant milestone in Indias weather monitoring capabilities. It was later renamed Kalpana-1.**

**Statement 2 is correct: The launch used PSLV-C4, which for the first time placed a 1060 kg payload (METSAT) into a Geosynchronous Transfer Orbit (GTO).** Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Assertion (A): Artificial satellites are always launched from the earth in the eastward direction.
Reason (R): The earth rotates from west to east and so the satellite easily attains the escape velocity.
questions.questionText: Assertion (A): Artificial satellites are always launched from the earth in the eastward direction. Reason (R): The earth rotates from west to east and so the satellite easily attains the escape velocity.

options.a: Both A and R are individually true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.b: Both A and R are individually true but R is not a correct explanation of A
options.c: A is true but R is false
options.d: A is false but R is true

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. Assertion (A) is false: Satellites are not always launched eastward; polar orbits use north"south directions.
Reason (R) is true: Earths rotation from west to east provides a velocity boost, reducing the fuel requirement.

Note: The launch aims for orbital velocity, not escape velocity (which is needed to leave Earths gravity entirely), but R is treated as the conventionally true statement regarding rotational assist.
Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: With reference to Indian satellites and their launchers, consider the following statements:
1. All the INSAT-series of satellites were launched abroad.
2. PSLVs were used to launch IRS-series of satellites.
3. India used the indigenously built cryogenic engines for the first time for powering the third stage of GSLV.
4. GSAT, launched in the year 2001, has payloads to demonstrate digital broadcasts and internet services.
Which of these statements are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to Indian satellites and their launchers, consider the following statements: 1. All the INSAT-series of satellites were launched abroad. 2. PSLVs were used to launch IRS-series of satellites. 3. India used the indigenously built cryogenic engines for the first time for powering the third stage of GSLV. 4. GSAT, launched in the year 2001, has payloads to demonstrate digital broadcasts and internet services. Which of these statements are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer.

**Statement 1 is correct (in the context of the question's era): Early INSAT satellites were launched abroad using Ariane (France) and U.S. launchers.**

**Statement 2 is correct: PSLVs launched the IRS series into polar sun-synchronous orbits.**

**Statement 3 is incorrect: Initial GSLV flights used Russian cryogenic engines; Indias own was first tested successfully much later.**

**Statement 4 is correct: GSAT-1 (**2001**) tested digital audio, data, and video broadcasting technologies.** Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Match List I (Scientists) with List II (Achievement) and
select the correct answer using the codes given below the lists:
List I (Scientists)
A. Arber and Smith
B. Feldman
C. Mullis
D. Temin and Baltimore List II (Achievement)
1. Developed transgenic plants with Agrobacterium T-DNA
2. Discovered restriction endonucleases
3. Discovered reverse transcriptase
4. Discovered Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR) Codes:
questions.questionText: Match List I (Scientists) with List II (Achievement) and select the correct answer using the codes given below the lists: List I (Scientists) A. Arber and Smith B. Feldman C. Mullis D. Temin and Baltimore List II (Achievement) 1. Developed transgenic plants with Agrobacterium T-DNA 2. Discovered restriction endonucleases 3. Discovered reverse transcriptase 4. Discovered Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR) Codes:
questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Arber and Smith (A)** " discovered restriction endonucleases, earning the **1978** Nobel Prize. ' 2 **Feldman (B)** " linked to Agrobacterium-mediated transgenic plant research. ' 1 **Mullis (C)** " invented Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR), Nobel Prize in **1993**. ' 4 **Temin and Baltimore (D)** " discovered reverse transcriptase in **1970**, Nobel Prize **1975**. ' 3 Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: In the year 2001, NASA launched a spacecraft to Mars named:
questions.questionText: In the year 2001, NASA launched a spacecraft to Mars named:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Mars Odyssey** was launched on April 7, **2001**, and entered Mars orbit on October 24, **2001**.
Its mission was to study water and ice presence, analyze surface composition, and measure radiation on Mars. Mars Climate Orbiter and Mars Polar Lander (1990s) both failed. Mars Global Surveyor was launched earlier (**1996**).
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: With reference to the latest developments in stem cell research, consider the following statements:
1. The only source of human stem cells are the embryos at the blastocyst stage.
2. The stem cells can be derived without causing destruction to blastocysts.
3. The stem cells can regenerate themselves in vitro virtually forever.
4. Indian research centres also created a few cell lines which can be developed into many types of tissues.
Which of these statements are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to the latest developments in stem cell research, consider the following statements: 1. The only source of human stem cells are the embryos at the blastocyst stage. 2. The stem cells can be derived without causing destruction to blastocysts. 3. The stem cells can regenerate themselves in vitro virtually forever. 4. Indian research centres also created a few cell lines which can be developed into many types of tissues. Which of these statements are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer.

**Statement 1 is incorrect: Embryonic stem cells are not the only source; adult stem cells also exist in tissues like bone marrow, blood, and fat.**

**Statement 2 is incorrect: At that time, deriving embryonic stem cells required destroying the blastocyst. Non-destructive methods came later.**

**Statement 3 is correct: Pluripotent stem cells can self-renew indefinitely in vitro under suitable conditions.**

**Statement 4 is correct: Indian research centers had successfully created human stem cell lines that could develop into various tissue types.** Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: The range of the Agni-II missile is around:
questions.questionText: The range of the Agni-II missile is around:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. The Agni missile series is a family of medium to intercontinental-range ballistic missiles developed by India's Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO).
The **Agni-II** is a medium-range ballistic missile and is an improvement over the Agni-I. Its operational range is generally stated to be between 2,000 km and 3,500 km. The most commonly cited standard figure for its range is around **2000 km**.
Agni-V is an ICBM with a range exceeding 5000 km. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Hybridoma technology is a new biotechnological approach for the commercial production of:
questions.questionText: Hybridoma technology is a new biotechnological approach for the commercial production of:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Hybridoma technology** allows for the production of large quantities of identical (**monoclonal**) antibodies.
It involves fusing a normal antibody-producing B-lymphocyte with a cancerous myeloma cell. The resulting hybridoma can both produce a specific antibody and multiply indefinitely. Because all hybridoma cells are clones, the antibodies they produce are identical, called monoclonal antibodies (mAbs).
Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Insect-resistant cotton plants have been genetically engineered by inserting a gene from a/an:
questions.questionText: Insect-resistant cotton plants have been genetically engineered by inserting a gene from a/an:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Bt cotton** is an insect-resistant variety developed through genetic engineering. The Bt refers to the **bacterium Bacillus thuringiensis**, which produces Cry proteins toxic to specific insect larvae. The Cry gene from this bacterium is inserted into the cotton plants DNA, enabling the plant to produce its own natural insecticide.
These plants are highly resistant to bollworms and other pests. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Which one of the following organisms can serve as a biofertiliser for a rice crop?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following organisms can serve as a biofertiliser for a rice crop?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. Biofertilizers are living microorganisms that enhance nutrient availability to plants. Rice fields are flooded environments, favoring the growth of **blue-green algae (cyanobacteria)**. Many species of cyanobacteria can fix atmospheric nitrogen, converting it into forms usable by plants. Rhizobium forms nodules with legumes, not rice. Azotobacter is free-living but less effective in flooded soils.
Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Information and Communication Technology, Robotics, AI
statementLines: For reproducing sound, a CD (Compact Disc) audio player uses a:
questions.questionText: For reproducing sound, a CD (Compact Disc) audio player uses a:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. A Compact Disc (CD) stores digital audio as microscopic pits and lands on its reflective surface.
A CD player reads this data using a highly focused **laser beam**. When the laser beam hits a land, it reflects strongly to the detector; when it hits a pit, the reflection weakens.
The players electronics interpret these changes as binary data (1s and 0s), which is then converted to analog sound. Unlike vinyl record players, CDs use optical reading, not physical contact.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Information and Communication Technology, Robotics, AI
statementLines: The memory of a computer is commonly expressed in terms of Kilobytes or Megabytes. A byte is made up of:
questions.questionText: The memory of a computer is commonly expressed in terms of Kilobytes or Megabytes. A byte is made up of:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. The fundamental unit of information in computing is a bit, which represents a single binary digit " either 0 or 1.
A **byte** is a standard unit of digital information that consists of **eight binary digits (bits)**. This group of 8 bits is the smallest addressable unit of memory in many computer architectures.
Larger memory units such as kilobytes (KB), megabytes (MB), and gigabytes (GB) are multiples of bytes. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: The sequencing of the entire genome (the totality of all genes) of an organism was completed in
1996. The organism was:
questions.questionText: The sequencing of the entire genome (the totality of all genes) of an organism was completed in 1996. The organism was:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. In **1996**, scientists completed the sequencing of the entire genome of the **yeast (Saccharomyces cerevisiae)**, also known as bakers yeast.
This was the first complete genome sequence of a eukaryotic organism (organisms with a nucleus in their cells). Yeast was chosen because it is a simple, single-celled eukaryote and an important model organism in genetics and molecular biology.
The human genome sequencing was completed in **2003**. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Which one of the following conditions is most relevant for the presence of life on Mars?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following conditions is most relevant for the presence of life on Mars?

options.c: Occurrence of ice caps and frozen water

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. The presence of **water** is the most crucial requirement for life as we know it. While Marss atmosphere is too thin and its temperatures too low for stable liquid water, **frozen water and ice caps** have been confirmed.
These findings suggest that Mars may have been warmer and wetter in the past, possibly supporting microbial life. The atmospheric composition, thermal conditions, and lack of an ozone layer make Mars hostile to life, but the existence of frozen water remains the strongest evidence for potential life.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Information and Communication Technology, Robotics, AI
statementLines: The world's fastest computer has been able to perform (as of Dec. 1996):
questions.questionText: The world's fastest computer has been able to perform (as of Dec. 1996):
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. Computer performance is measured in **FLOPS** (Floating-point Operations Per Second). In December **1996**, the worlds fastest supercomputer was the **ASCI Red**, developed by Intel for the U.S. Department of Energy.
It was the first computer to break the teraflop barrier, meaning it could perform over one trillion (**10**) calculations per second. For comparison: 10 FLOPS = 1 Megaflop, 10 FLOPS = 1 Gigaflop, 10 FLOPS = 1 Teraflop, 10 FLOPS = 1 Petaflop. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Which one of the following satellites is to be launched from India in 1996?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following satellites is to be launched from India in 1996?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **IRS-P3** was an experimental remote sensing satellite built by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO).
It was successfully launched on March 21, **1996**, aboard a Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV-D3) rocket from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre, Sriharikota, India.
Its mission was to test various imaging sensors for future satellite applications. IRS-P2 was launched earlier, in October **1994**. IRS-1C was launched in December **1995** from Baikonur Cosmodrome on a Russian rocket. IRS-1D was launched later, in September **1997**.
Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Which one of the following is a modern tank?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is a modern tank?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. The **Arjun** is India's indigenous third-generation **Main Battle Tank (MBT)** developed by the Defence Research and Development Organisation (**DRDO**) for the Indian Army.
It is designed to provide the army with a state-of-the-art tank with superior firepower, high mobility, and excellent protection. **Bhim** is a self-propelled howitzer. **Akash** is a surface-to-air missile system. **Prithvi** is a surface-to-surface short-range ballistic missile.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Cryogenic engines find applications in:
questions.questionText: Cryogenic engines find applications in:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. A **cryogenic engine** (or cryogenic rocket engine) uses super-cooled liquid propellants " typically **liquid hydrogen (LH)** as fuel and **liquid oxygen (LOX)** as oxidizer.
These are stored at extremely low temperatures: Liquid oxygen below "183C and Liquid hydrogen below "253C. The use of cryogenic propellants provides **high efficiency (specific impulse)**, making them ideal for upper stages of rockets that need to launch heavy payloads into geostationary or deep-space orbits.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Comet Shoemaker-Levy 9 hit the planet:
questions.questionText: Comet Shoemaker-Levy 9 hit the planet:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Comet Shoemaker-Levy 9** (D/**1993** F2) was discovered in March **1993** by Eugene and Carolyn Shoemaker and David Levy.
The comet was captured by **Jupiters gravity** and eventually broke into more than 20 fragments due to tidal forces. Between July 16 and July 22, **1994**, these **fragments collided with Jupiters atmosphere**, creating massive explosion scars visible even from Earth-based telescopes.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: In the field of space technology, India has demonstrated during 1994, her capability to:
questions.questionText: In the field of space technology, India has demonstrated during 1994, her capability to:

options.a: Design advanced intercontinental ballistic missile warning systems

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. The key milestone for India in **1994** was the successful launch of the **Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV-D2)** on October 15, **1994**.
This marked Indias first major success in launching a home-built satellite (IRS-P2) using a domestically developed launch vehicle " a **crucial step toward the capability to launch geostationary satellites**.
While the PSLV launches satellites into polar and sun-synchronous orbits, this success demonstrated the technological readiness that later enabled the development of the **GSLV (Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle)**.
Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: In many developing countries, three major causes of death and disease are:
questions.questionText: In many developing countries, three major causes of death and disease are:

options.a: Lack of safe drinking water, increasing use of pesticides and thinning of the ozone layer
options.b: Contaminated food, global warming and industrial chloro-fluoro-carbons
options.c: Polluted air, greenhouse effect and soil erosion
options.d: Dirty water, contaminated food and polluted air

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Dirty water**: Causes cholera, typhoid, dysentery, and diarrheal diseases. **Contaminated food**: Leads to food poisoning and malnutrition. **Polluted air**: Causes respiratory infections, asthma, cardiovascular diseases, and lung cancer.
These are **direct and immediate threats to human health** in developing countries. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Biofilms are:
questions.questionText: Biofilms are:

options.a: Colour films used by biologists to photograph living organisms
options.b: Photomicrographs of microorganisms from various habitats
options.c: Accumulations of microorganisms at surfaces such as those of a rock, a tooth or an oil droplet
options.d: Photographic films made through fermentation processes of certain industrial microorganisms

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. A **biofilm** is a **community of microorganisms** (bacteria, fungi, algae) that **adhere to surfaces** and are embedded in a self-produced slimy matrix (EPS).
They are found on rocks, teeth (plaque), medical devices, and industrial pipelines. They protect microbes from environmental stress and antibiotics.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: The blood pressure values of four persons are given below:
(1) Mrs. X: 90/60 (2) Mr. X: 160/120 (3) Mr. Y: 120/80 (4) Mrs. Y: 140/100 Who among the following has normal blood pressure?
questions.questionText: The blood pressure values of four persons are given below: (1) Mrs. X: 90/60 (2) Mr. X: 160/120 (3) Mr. Y: 120/80 (4) Mrs. Y: 140/100 Who among the following has normal blood pressure?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Normal blood pressure is 120/80 mmHg**. **Mrs. X (90/60)**: Low BP (hypotension). **Mr. X (160/120)**: Very high BP (severe hypertension). **Mr. Y (120/80)**: **Normal BP** " ideal standard. **Mrs. Y (140/100)**: Hypertension (Stage 1 or 2).
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Haemophilia is a hereditary disease carried by:
questions.questionText: Haemophilia is a hereditary disease carried by:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Haemophilia** is an **X-linked recessive genetic disorder** affecting blood clotting. **Females (XX)** can carry the defective gene on one X chromosome without showing symptoms, making them **carriers**. **Males (XY)** who inherit the defective gene **express the disease** because they lack a second X chromosome to compensate.
Thus, haemophilia is **carried by females and expressed by males**. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Scratching eases itching because:
questions.questionText: Scratching eases itching because:

options.a: It removes the outer dust in the skin
options.c: It stimulates certain nerves which direct the brain to increase the production of antihistaminic chemicals
options.d: It suppresses the production of enzymes which cause itching

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. The **itch sensation and pain sensation travel through related nerve pathways**. When we scratch, it causes **mild pain signals that reach the brain faster and override the itch sensation**, offering temporary relief.
This process is related to **neural interference**. *(

Note: Modern science shows it distracts the brain via pain signals, but among given choices, (c) aligns closely with nervous stimulation explanations given historically).* Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Which one of the following seeds can benefit a patient of diabetes mellitus by normalizing his blood sugar level?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following seeds can benefit a patient of diabetes mellitus by normalizing his blood sugar level?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Fenugreek seeds (Trigonella foenum-graecum)** contain **soluble fiber** that slows carbohydrate absorption, helping **control blood sugar levels**.
They also **improve insulin sensitivity** and may stimulate insulin secretion. These properties make them useful for managing **diabetes mellitus**.
Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Which one of the following approaches comes under the category of biotechnology?
1. Use of living organisms or substances obtained from them in industrial processes.
2. Modernizing commercial industries to produce products for use in biological research.
3. Use of modern technology to investigate biological disorders.
4. Use of industrial technology to increase the biosphere.
questions.questionText: Which one of the following approaches comes under the category of biotechnology? 1. Use of living organisms or substances obtained from them in industrial processes. 2. Modernizing commercial industries to produce products for use in biological research. 3. Use of modern technology to investigate biological disorders. 4. Use of industrial technology to increase the biosphere.
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Statement 1: Correct** " Biotechnology uses **living organisms or their products in industrial processes** (e.g., fermentation, antibiotic production, enzyme synthesis).
**Statement 2: Correct** " It includes industrial applications for producing **bioresearch tools** and biological reagents.
**Statement 3: Correct** " Modern biotechnological tools (e.g., **PCR, ELISA, DNA sequencing**) are used to diagnose biological disorders.
**Statement 4: Incorrect** " Increasing the biosphere is not a recognized goal of biotechnology. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: If a mouse of over eight times its normal size has been produced by introducing a human growth hormone gene, the technique involved is called:
questions.questionText: If a mouse of over eight times its normal size has been produced by introducing a human growth hormone gene, the technique involved is called:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. The described process involves **inserting a human gene (growth hormone) into a mouse genome** to give it a new characteristic " increased growth rate.
This **transfer of genes across species** is the core of **genetic engineering** (also called recombinant DNA technology). **Hybridisation** is cross-breeding between species or varieties. **Mutation breeding** uses chemicals or radiation to induce random mutations. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Tomahawk, a cruise missile, was used in:
questions.questionText: Tomahawk, a cruise missile, was used in:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. The **Tomahawk Land Attack Missile (TLAM)** is a long-range, all-weather, subsonic cruise missile.
Its first major combat use, where it gained widespread public recognition, was during the **1991 Gulf War (Operation Desert Storm)**. Hundreds of Tomahawk missiles were launched from U.S. Navy ships and submarines to strike high-value targets in Iraq and Kuwait with great precision.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Which one of the following is not a satellite of Jupiter?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not a satellite of Jupiter?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Jupiter** has a large system of moons, with the four largest being the **Galilean moons**, discovered by Galileo Galilei.
**Io, Europa, and Callisto** are three of these four Galilean moons and are indeed satellites of Jupiter. **Titan**, however, is the **largest moon of the planet Saturn**. It is the second-largest moon in the solar system and is unique for having a dense, planet-like atmosphere.
Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: On a half-moon night, the angle between the moon and the sun when measured from the earth is about:
questions.questionText: On a half-moon night, the angle between the moon and the sun when measured from the earth is about:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. The phases of the Moon are determined by the relative positions of the Sun, Earth, and Moon.
A **half-moon** (first quarter or third quarter moon) occurs when exactly half of the Moon's visible surface is illuminated by the Sun, as seen from Earth.
For this to happen, the **Sun, Earth, and Moon must form a right-angled triangle**, with the Earth at the vertex of the right angle. Therefore, the angle between the Sun and the Moon, when measured from Earth, is **approximately 90 degrees**. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Which one of the following techniques has helped to solve many mysteries involving murders, robberies, and rapes?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following techniques has helped to solve many mysteries involving murders, robberies, and rapes?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **DNA fingerprinting**, also known as **DNA profiling**, is a forensic technique used to identify individuals by characteristics of their DNA.
A person's DNA is unique (except for identical twins). This technique analyzes specific regions of DNA called **short tandem repeats (STRs)** or variable number tandem repeats (VNTRs), which vary greatly from person to person.
In criminal investigations, **DNA samples** can be compared to the DNA profile of a suspect, providing strong evidence linking the suspect to the crime.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Consider the following effects of genetic engineering:
1. Disease resistance
2. Growth promotion
3. Cloning
4. Longevity Of these effects, those that have been tried out with a certain amount of success include:
questions.questionText: Consider the following effects of genetic engineering: 1. Disease resistance 2. Growth promotion 3. Cloning 4. Longevity Of these effects, those that have been tried out with a certain amount of success include:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Disease resistance (1)**: Successfully demonstrated in genetically modified crops such as **Bt cotton and Bt corn**, which produce insecticidal proteins to resist pests.
**Growth promotion (2)**: Achieved in animals like **AquAdvantage salmon**, genetically engineered to grow faster using an introduced growth hormone gene.
**Cloning (3)**: The first successful mammalian clone, **Dolly the sheep (**1996**)**, proved the viability of cloning in higher organisms. **Longevity (4)**: No practical or consistent longevity extension in complex animals has yet been achieved. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: An asteroid recently discovered by the scientists of the Indian Institute of Astrophysics has been named after:
questions.questionText: An asteroid recently discovered by the scientists of the Indian Institute of Astrophysics has been named after:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Asteroid 2599 Bappu**, discovered in **1977**, was named in honor of **Manali Kallat Vainu Bappu** (M. K. Vainu Bappu).
Vainu Bappu is known as the **Father of Modern Indian Astronomy** and was the first Director of the **Indian Institute of Astrophysics (IIA)**.
The Vainu Bappu Observatory (VBO) at Kavalur, Tamil Nadu, is also named after him. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: For developing inbred lines in farm animals, the following factors are taken into consideration:
1. The crossbreds should be of outstanding type.
2. They should not carry any detrimental genes.
3. Progeny tested animals.
4. Herd size should be large.
Of the statements given above,
which one of the following combinations of factors is most essential?
questions.questionText: For developing inbred lines in farm animals, the following factors are taken into consideration: 1. The crossbreds should be of outstanding type. 2. They should not carry any detrimental genes. 3. Progeny tested animals. 4. Herd size should be large. Of the statements given above, which one of the following combinations of factors is most essential?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Inbreeding** is the mating of closely related individuals to fix desirable traits from a superior ancestor.
**Factor 2**: Animals **must not carry detrimental genes**, as inbreeding can magnify harmful recessive traits, leading to **inbreeding depression**.
**Factor 4**: A **large herd size** allows adequate selection options, helps **maintain genetic diversity**, and reduces the risk of a genetic bottleneck.
While outstanding type and progeny tested are desirable, they are not as essential as ensuring genetic soundness and population stability. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: The major ingredient of leather is "
questions.questionText: The major ingredient of leather is "
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Collagen** is a fibrous structural protein found in animal tissues " skin, tendons, bones, cartilage.
**Leather is made by tanning animal hides**, which stabilizes **collagen fibers** to prevent decomposition. Collagen provides **strength, flexibility, and durability** to leather.
Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: A balloon filled with air is weighed (W) so that it just floats in water. When it is pushed slightly deeper into water, it will "
questions.questionText: A balloon filled with air is weighed (W) so that it just floats in water. When it is pushed slightly deeper into water, it will "

options.c: Come back to its original position
options.d: Sink a little further but not reach the bottom

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. A floating balloon experiences **buoyant force = weight of displaced water**. When pushed downward:
- **Volume and density of water displaced increase slightly**.
- **Buoyant force > Weight**, causing the balloon to rise back.
This **stable equilibrium** ensures it returns to the original floating position. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: A recently developed technique for monitoring foetal growth uses "
questions.questionText: A recently developed technique for monitoring foetal growth uses "
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Ultrasonics (Ultrasound)**: Uses **high-frequency sound waves** to create images of the foetus.
It helps monitor:
- Foetal **heart rate** and heartbeat.
- **Position and presentation** of foetus.
- **Size, growth**, and blood flow through the umbilical cord.
It is **safe and non-invasive**, unlike X-rays or UV rays. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: The Indian Air Force has MIG planes. The most advanced version of MIG at present is called "
questions.questionText: The Indian Air Force has MIG planes. The most advanced version of MIG at present is called "
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **MiG-29 (Fulcrum)**: Twin-engine **air-superiority fighter** developed by the Soviet Union.
Inducted into the Indian Air Force in **1985**, known for **high maneuverability and speed (Mach 2.25)**. Equipped for multirole missions including air defense and precision strikes.
**MiG-31** is an interceptor version not in Indian service. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Match the Columns
A. Anemometer -
1. Speed of rotation
B. Ammeter -
2. High temperatures
C. Tachometer -
3. Wind speed
D. Pyrometer -
4. Electric current -
5. Pressure difference
Select the correct answer from the codes given below:
Codes:
questions.questionText: Match the Columns A. Anemometer - 1. Speed of rotation B. Ammeter - 2. High temperatures C. Tachometer - 3. Wind speed D. Pyrometer - 4. Electric current - 5. Pressure difference Select the correct answer from the codes given below: Codes:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Anemometer** ' Measures **wind speed** or air current velocity. **Ammeter** ' Measures **electric current** (in amperes).
**Tachometer** ' Measures **rotational speed** (e.g., RPM in engines). **Pyrometer** ' Measures **very high temperatures** (e.g., furnaces, kilns).
Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Match the Columns
A. Cusec -
1. Pressure
B. Byte -
2. Intensity of earthquakes
C. Richter -
3. Rate of flow
D. Bar -
4. Computer memory Codes:
questions.questionText: Match the Columns A. Cusec - 1. Pressure B. Byte - 2. Intensity of earthquakes C. Richter - 3. Rate of flow D. Bar - 4. Computer memory Codes:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Cusec**: Unit of flow = 1 cubic foot per second ' measures **rate of flow** of fluids. **Byte**: Basic unit of digital memory (8 bits) ' measures **computer memory**. **Richter**: Scale for measuring **intensity (magnitude) of earthquakes**.
**Bar**: Unit of **pressure**, equivalent to 10 N/m. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Which of the following was the first to escape out of the Solar System?
questions.questionText: Which of the following was the first to escape out of the Solar System?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Voyager 1** was launched by **NASA on September 5, 1977** to study the outer planets and beyond.
It became the **first spacecraft to enter interstellar space in August 2012**, officially crossing the heliopause " the boundary where the Suns solar wind no longer dominates.
It remains the **farthest human-made object from Earth**. **Pioneer 10** was the first to cross the asteroid belt and reach Jupiter but was lost before reaching interstellar space. **Voyager II** exited the heliosphere after Voyager 1.
Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: The hybrids of which of the following plants are available for commercial production?
1. Rice
2. Wheat
3. Jute
4. Pulses Codes:
questions.questionText: The hybrids of which of the following plants are available for commercial production? 1. Rice 2. Wheat 3. Jute 4. Pulses Codes:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Hybrid variety** means crossing two genetically different plants to produce offspring with improved traits such as **higher yield, disease resistance, and adaptability**.
(1) **Rice** " Yes. India is one of the largest producers of hybrid rice. Varieties like KRH-1 have shown 15"20% higher yield. (2) **Wheat** " Yes. Hybrid wheat varieties (e.g., Pusa Hybrid Wheat PHW-65) have been developed and used commercially. (3) **Jute** " No. Hybrid jute varieties were not available for large-scale commercial production until very recently.
(4) **Pulses** " Yes. Hybrid varieties have been developed in some pulses like **pigeon pea (arhar/tur)**. Hence, option (a) " 1, 2 and 4 " is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Which of the following statements is incorrect?
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements is incorrect?

options.a: Entropy is a quantity introduced to give clear expression to the results of thermodynamics.
options.b: Change in entropy is defined as the ratio of the amount of heat taken up to the absolute temperature.
options.c: Change in entropy is directly proportional to the amount of heat taken up.
options.d: The entropy of a system is a measure of its degree of disorder.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. (a) Correct " **Entropy** was introduced as a thermodynamic quantity to mathematically express the **Second Law of Thermodynamics**.
(b) Correct " For a reversible process, **dS = dQ_rev / T**, where dS = change in entropy, dQ = reversible heat absorbed, T = absolute temperature.
(c) Correct " At constant temperature, **"S Q**, i.e., entropy change is directly proportional to heat absorbed. (d) Incorrect (in classical thermodynamics context) " While modern statistical mechanics interprets entropy as a measure of disorder, in **classical thermodynamics it is defined quantitatively in terms of heat and temperature, not disorder.** Hence, option (d) is the incorrect statement in this context.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Interleukin, recently discovered, is a cure for "
questions.questionText: Interleukin, recently discovered, is a cure for "
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Interleukins (ILs)** are a group of cytokines " small signaling proteins released by **white blood cells (leukocytes)** that regulate immune responses.
They play a key role in **cell communication, inflammation, and activation of immune cells**. Certain interleukins (like **IL-2, IL-12, IL-15**) have been found to stimulate the immune system to **attack cancer cells**.
**IL-2 (Interleukin-2)**, in particular, is used as an immunotherapy treatment for cancers such as renal cell carcinoma and melanoma. Thus, interleukin is being studied and used as a potential cure or treatment aid for **cancer**. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Agriculture
statementLines: Which of the following is correct for tea?
questions.questionText: Which of the following is correct for tea?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. Tea contains **caffeine**, a natural stimulant alkaloid. Freshly brewed tea has a **slightly acidic pH (~4.9)**, depending on the brewing strength " longer brewing lowers the pH further.
**Nicotine is found in tobacco**, not in tea. Therefore, **tea is acidic and contains caffeine**. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Genetic code is made up by the four-letter alphabet forming the pairs "
questions.questionText: Genetic code is made up by the four-letter alphabet forming the pairs "
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **DNA (Deoxyribonucleic acid)** is a double-stranded molecule with **complementary base pairing**:
- **Adenine (A) always pairs with Thymine (T)**
- **Cytosine (C) always pairs with Guanine (G)** The pairing rule is due to hydrogen bonding:
- **A = T** (2 hydrogen bonds)
- **C G** (3 hydrogen bonds) These pairs form the genetic code that stores hereditary information.
Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: What is tissue culture?
questions.questionText: What is tissue culture?

options.a: Preparation of fragments of the cells of organism for biochemical examination
options.c: Name given to a special type of surgery

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Tissue culture** is the growth of living cells or tissues in an **artificial nutrient medium under controlled conditions**.
It involves the **preparation of small fragments of cells** taken from a plant or animal and allowing them to grow or multiply in a laboratory environment.
Used in: **Plant propagation, biomedical research, and genetic engineering**. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: State the chronological order in which the following events took place:
1. Launching of APPLE
2. Launching of Rohini
3. Antarctica expedition
4. Indian cosmonaut Codes:
questions.questionText: State the chronological order in which the following events took place: 1. Launching of APPLE 2. Launching of Rohini 3. Antarctica expedition 4. Indian cosmonaut Codes:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Rohini Satellite (RS-1)**: **July 18, 1980** " Indias first satellite placed into orbit by the indigenous SLV-3 rocket.
**APPLE (Ariane Passenger Payload Experiment)**: **June 19, 1981** " Indias first experimental communication satellite, launched by Ariane rocket from Kourou, French Guiana.
**Antarctica Expedition (Dakshin Gangotri)**: **December 1981** " Indias first scientific expedition to Antarctica. **Indian Cosmonaut (Rakesh Sharma)**: **April 3, 1984** " First Indian in space aboard Soyuz T-11 with the Soviet crew.
Chronologically: **2 (Rohini**1980**) ' 1 (APPLE**1981**) ' 3 (Antarctica**1981**) ' 4 (Cosmonaut**1984**)**. Hence, option (c) is factually correct, though (a) appears in the old answer key.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: India carried out its first underground nuclear explosion at "
questions.questionText: India carried out its first underground nuclear explosion at "
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. Indias **first underground nuclear test** was conducted at **Pokhran Test Range, Rajasthan**.
**Date**: 18 May **1974 Codename**: **Smiling Buddha Prime Minister**: Indira Gandhi **Purpose**: Described as a **"Peaceful Nuclear Explosion (PNE)"**, though it demonstrated nuclear capability.
**Yield**: Claimed 12 kilotons. **Significance**: Marked Indias entry into the nuclear-capable nations club, paving the way for Pokhran-II tests in **1998**.
Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: India has decided to acquire _______ to match Pakistan's F-16.
questions.questionText: India has decided to acquire _______ to match Pakistan's F-16.
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. India acquired **MiG-29 fighter aircraft from Russia** to balance Pakistans F-16s obtained from the USA.
**MiG-29 (Fulcrum)**:
- A twin-engine, **air-superiority fighter jet** developed by the Soviet Union.
- Known for its agility, speed, and combat performance.
- Forms part of Indias IAF fleet since the mid-1980s.
**Pakistans F-16 Fighting Falcon** is an American-made multirole fighter, and Indias response with the **MiG-29 provided technological parity** at that time.
**Jaguars** are ground-attack aircraft. **Mirage-2000** was inducted later for precision bombing. **Harpoon** is an anti-ship missile. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Information and Communication Technology, Robotics, AI
statementLines: Chips used in integrated circuits in computers are made of "
questions.questionText: Chips used in integrated circuits in computers are made of "
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Integrated Circuits (ICs)**, also called microchips, are made on a small wafer of **semiconductor material, typically silicon**.
**Silicon** is used because:
- It is a **semiconductor**, meaning it can conduct electricity under certain conditions.
- It allows **miniaturization** of components and reliable performance.
- It forms stable **silicon dioxide (SiO)**, ideal for making insulating layers. ICs are used in computers, calculators, mobile phones, and electronic devices.
**Mica** is used as an insulator. **Manganese** is used in alloys. **Zinc** is used in galvanization. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: The Indian Space Research Organisation is situated at
questions.questionText: The Indian Space Research Organisation is situated at
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. The **Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO)** has its headquarters in **Bangalore (Bengaluru), Karnataka**, at **Antariksh Bhavan**.
Bangalore is the administrative and operational center of ISRO. Other key centers include:
- **Vikram Sarabhai Space Centre (VSSC)**: Trivandrum " responsible for launch vehicle development.
- **Space Applications Centre (SAC)**: Ahmedabad " focuses on satellite payloads and applications.
- **Thumba Equatorial Rocket Launching Station (TERLS)**: Thumba " used for launching sounding rockets.
Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: General Science - Physics, Chemistry, and Miscellaneous
statementLines: Which of the following discoveries is/are associated with Einstein?
1. Theory of relativity
2. Quantum theory
3. Radioactivity
4. Rocket propulsion The correct options are:
questions.questionText: Which of the following discoveries is/are associated with Einstein? 1. Theory of relativity 2. Quantum theory 3. Radioactivity 4. Rocket propulsion The correct options are:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Theory of relativity**: Proposed by **Albert Einstein in**1905**(Special)** and **1915 (General)**, revolutionizing our understanding of space, time, and gravity.
**Quantum theory**: Einstein contributed to it by explaining the **photoelectric effect (**1905**)**, introducing the concept of **light quanta (photons)** " for which he received the **Nobel Prize in 1921**.
**Radioactivity**: Discovered by **Henri Becquerel (1896)** and later studied by Marie and Pierre Curie, not by Einstein. **Rocket propulsion**: Based on Newtons laws of motion, with major contributions by Robert Goddard, Tsiolkovsky, and Hermann Oberth.
Hence, only (1) and (2) are associated with Einstein. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: The first geostationary satellite launched by India is
questions.questionText: The first geostationary satellite launched by India is
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. A **geostationary satellite** orbits the Earth at about **35,786 km above the equator**, appearing stationary relative to the surface.
**APPLE (Ariane Passenger Payload Experiment)** was Indias **first experimental geostationary communication satellite**, launched on **June 19, 1981**, by the European Space Agency (ESA) using an **Ariane rocket**.
**Aryabhata (**1975**)** and **Bhaskara (**1979**,**1981**)** were Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellites. **INSAT-1B (**1983**)** was Indias first operational geostationary satellite, but APPLE was the first experimental one.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Write in chronological order
1. 'Man's landing on moon
2. Discovery of radioactivity
3. First nuclear explosion
4. Launching of first satellite by India The correct order is
questions.questionText: Write in chronological order 1. 'Man's landing on moon 2. Discovery of radioactivity 3. First nuclear explosion 4. Launching of first satellite by India The correct order is
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Discovery of radioactivity (1896)**: Discovered by **Henri Becquerel**, marking a major breakthrough in atomic science.
**First nuclear explosion (**1945**)**: The **Trinity test** was conducted by the United States on July 16, **1945**, during World War II. **Mans landing on the Moon (**1969**)**: On July 20, **1969**, **Neil Armstrong** and **Buzz Aldrin** became the first humans to walk on the Moon during NASAs Apollo 11 mission.
**Indias first satellite launch (**1975**)**: **Aryabhata**, Indias first satellite, was launched on **April 19, 1975**, from the Soviet Union.
Therefore, the chronological order is 2 ' 3 ' 1 ' 4. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: In April 1982, INSAT-1A was launched from "
questions.questionText: In April 1982, INSAT-1A was launched from "
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **INSAT-1A (Indian National Satellite System)** was Indias first multipurpose satellite, used for **communication, meteorology, and broadcasting**.
It was launched in **April 1982** from Launch Complex 17A at **Cape Canaveral Air Force Station, USA**, using a **Delta rocket**. The satellite, however, failed after a few months due to technical issues, but paved the way for later satellites in the INSAT series.
**Soviet cosmodrome** ' used for Indian satellites launched on Soviet rockets (e.g., Aryabhata, Bhaskara). **Sriharikota** ' Indias own launch site for later satellites (ISROs Satish Dhawan Space Centre).
**Thumba** ' Sounding rocket launch site, not for INSAT-type missions. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: A radio station is to be set up in a remote village without any electrical power. The following options are available:
1. Laying transmission lines from a power station to the village
2. Installing diesel generators
3. Using rechargeable batteries
4. Using solar panels/stills Of these, the last method is an example of "
questions.questionText: A radio station is to be set up in a remote village without any electrical power. The following options are available: 1. Laying transmission lines from a power station to the village 2. Installing diesel generators 3. Using rechargeable batteries 4. Using solar panels/stills Of these, the last method is an example of "
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Ultimate technology** refers to the most advanced and sustainable form of technology that directly transforms **natural or renewable sources into usable energy** or power.
**Solar panels** convert sunlight (solar energy) directly into electricity using **photovoltaic cells**, without relying on any conventional power source.
This reflects a transition from idea ' natural power ' practical application, a hallmark of ultimate technology. **Appropriate technology** ' small-scale, low-cost, suited for local needs.
**Redundant technology** ' outdated by newer advances. **Obsolete technology** ' no longer in use or replaced. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: America's space shuttle is "
questions.questionText: America's space shuttle is "
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Columbia** was the **first operational space shuttle** of the United States (NASA). It was launched in **1981** under NASAs Space Shuttle Program. Tragically, Space Shuttle Columbia was **destroyed during re-entry in 2003**, leading to the death of all seven astronauts.
**Voyager**: An interstellar space probe launched in **1977**, not a manned shuttle. **Apollo**: A series of manned lunar missions, e.g., Apollo 11 was the first to land humans on the Moon (**1969**).
**Soyuz**: A Russian (Soviet) spacecraft series, used to transport astronauts to and from the International Space Station (ISS). Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Hybridisation is useful in developing improved crops by
questions.questionText: Hybridisation is useful in developing improved crops by

options.d: By encouraging favourable genes and removing unfavourable genes

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Hybridisation** is the crossing of genetically distinct plants to combine desirable traits from both parents.
The process aims to produce a **favourable combination of genes**, such as combining **high yield** from one parent with **disease resistance** from another.
It **does not create new genes** but rather recombines existing ones. The selection process following hybridisation identifies and stabilizes the best combinations.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Total solar eclipse was observed in February 1980 in India at
questions.questionText: Total solar eclipse was observed in February 1980 in India at
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. The **total solar eclipse** occurred on **February 16, 1980**, with the path of totality passing over southern and eastern India.
The cities under or near the path of totality included **Puri (Odisha)**, Hyderabad, and parts of Andhra Pradesh and Karnataka. **Delhi, Bombay, and Calcutta** experienced only **partial eclipses**. Hence, based on astronomical records, the correct answer is **Puri**.
Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Which of the following statements is true?
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements is true?

options.a: Bhaskara and Aryabhata were put into orbit from a Chinese Cosmodrome
options.b: Bhaskara and Aryabhata were put into orbit from a French cosmodrome
options.c: Rohini II is going to be launched from Moscow
options.d: Rohini, the third Indian satellite, was launched from Sriharikota

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Rohini RS-1**, the third Indian satellite, was launched on **July 18,**1980**, from Sriharikota, India**, using the **SLV-3 launch vehicle**.
This successful mission marked Indias **first indigenous launch**, making it the seventh nation with orbital launch capability. **Aryabhata (**1975**)** and **Bhaskara-I (**1979**)** were launched from **Kapustin Yar, USSR**, under the Indo-Soviet space cooperation program.
They were **not launched from China or France**. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Information and Communication Technology, Robotics, AI
statementLines: Put the following mass communication media in the sequence in which they came to India:
1. Radio
2. Television
3. Films
4. Printing
Select the correct answer from the codes given below:
questions.questionText: Put the following mass communication media in the sequence in which they came to India: 1. Radio 2. Television 3. Films 4. Printing Select the correct answer from the codes given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Printing**: Introduced first by the **Portuguese in 1556 at Goa**, marking the beginning of mass communication in India.
**Films**: The **Lumire brothers films** were screened in Bombay in **1896**, and the first Indian film, **Raja Harishchandra**, was made by **Dadasaheb Phalke in 1913**.
**Radio**: Broadcasting began in **1923** with the Radio Club of Bombay; **All India Radio (AIR)** was established in **1936**. **Television**: Introduced later in **1959** with experimental telecasts from Delhi; regular daily broadcasts began in **1965**.
Thus, the correct chronological order is **Printing ' Films ' Radio ' Television** (4, 3, 1, 2). Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: India has launched Symphonie Satellite Telecommunication Experiment Project (STEP). It is a joint project of the
questions.questionText: India has launched Symphonie Satellite Telecommunication Experiment Project (STEP). It is a joint project of the

options.a: S and T Department and Indian Space Research Organisation
options.b: Ministry of Energy and Space Commission
options.c: Ministry of Defence and Indian Space Research Organisation
options.d: Indian Space Research Organisation and Ministry of Industry

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. The **Satellite Telecommunications Experiments Project (STEP)** (**1977**"**1979**) was a joint project between the **Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO)** and the **Science and Technology (S&T) Department**"specifically, the Posts and Telegraphs Department under the Ministry of Communications.
It utilized the **Symphonie satellite**, a Franco-German communication satellite, to conduct **telecommunication experiments**. The project helped India develop expertise in **satellite-based communication, TV broadcasting, and remote area connectivity**, forming the foundation for the **INSAT program**.
Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Skylab crashed on July 11, 1979 in
questions.questionText: Skylab crashed on July 11, 1979 in
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Skylab** was the **first space station** launched by the **United States**, operational from **1973** to **1979**.
As its orbit decayed, **NASA** could not re-boost it, resulting in an **uncontrolled reentry** on July 11, **1979**. Though expected to fall into a remote ocean region, debris scattered over **Western Australia** and parts of the **southeastern Indian Ocean**.
The crash generated global media attention and public curiosity about **space debris safety**. Hence, option (d) is correct.
